services.main_service_name: Apply for arts and cultural industry funding
services.name: Apply for an arts project or individual artist grant
services.desc: Artists and arts organizations can apply for project grants, operating grants, and individual artist grants through the Alberta Foundation for the Arts. Funding supports visual arts, performing arts, literary arts, and media arts across the province.
services.users: Individual artists, arts organizations, arts collectives

services.main_service_name: Apply for arts and cultural industry funding
services.name: Raise funds through online crowdfunding
services.desc: Non-profit and community organizations can use the government-backed CrowdFunding Alberta platform to raise funds from the public for community projects, arts initiatives, and social causes.
services.users: Non-profit organizations, community groups, social enterprises

services.main_service_name: Produce, classify, and distribute films
services.name: Apply for film and television production funding
services.desc: Film and television producers can apply for production grants to support screen-based productions in Alberta. Funding supports job creation and economic activity in the creative industries.
services.users: Film producers, television producers, digital media creators

services.main_service_name: Produce, classify, and distribute films
services.name: Submit a film or video for classification
services.desc: All films, videos, and video games distributed or exhibited in Alberta must be classified. Distributors submit content for rating by Alberta's film classification office, which assigns age-appropriate classifications to protect consumers.
services.users: Film distributors, exhibitors, video game distributors

relationships.service: Get information about the Fairness and Safety in Sport Act

services.main_service_name: Produce, classify, and distribute films
services.name: Apply for a film distribution licence
services.desc: Anyone who distributes films in Alberta must hold a valid distribution licence issued under the Film and Video Classification Act. Licences must be renewed annually.

services.main_service_name: Learn about arts profession recognition
services.name: Learn about your rights as a professional artist
services.desc: Alberta recognizes the professional status of artists through legislation that establishes principles for fair treatment of arts professionals and affirms their contribution to the social, cultural, and economic well-being of the province.
services.users: Professional artists, arts organizations, cultural workers

services.main_service_name: Apply for community and non-profit grants
services.name: Apply for a community project operating grant
services.desc: Non-profit organizations, Indigenous communities, and other eligible groups can apply for operating grants to support community projects that improve quality of life. Grants fund programs in areas including social services, culture, sports, and community development.
services.users: Non-profit organizations, Indigenous communities, school councils

services.main_service_name: Apply for community and non-profit grants
services.name: Apply for a community facility improvement grant
services.desc: Non-profit organizations, municipalities, and Indigenous communities can apply for capital grants to build, renovate, or upgrade community-use facilities such as community halls, playgrounds, sports fields, and cultural centres.

services.main_service_name: Visit heritage sites, museums, and archives
services.name: Visit a provincial museum or historic site
services.desc: Alberta operates the Royal Alberta Museum and Royal Tyrrell Museum of Palaeontology, along with historic sites and interpretive centres across the province. These institutions preserve and share Alberta's natural and cultural heritage.

services.main_service_name: Visit heritage sites, museums, and archives
services.name: Access the Provincial Archives
services.desc: The Provincial Archives of Alberta collects, preserves, and provides access to records documenting Alberta's history. Researchers and the public can search collections, request records, and visit the reading room.
services.users: Researchers, genealogists, historians, students, general public

services.main_service_name: Protect and designate heritage resources
services.name: Apply for heritage resource designation
services.desc: Property owners, municipalities, and organizations can apply for provincial or municipal historic resource designation to protect significant buildings, structures, or sites. Designation provides legal protection and may qualify the owner for conservation grants.

services.main_service_name: Protect and designate heritage resources
services.name: Apply for an archaeological or palaeontological research permit
services.desc: Researchers must obtain a permit before conducting archaeological or palaeontological fieldwork in Alberta. Permits ensure proper scientific standards and protection of heritage resources.

services.main_service_name: Access French-language services
services.name: Access French-language government services and supports
services.desc: Alberta's Francophone Secretariat coordinates French-language services across government, ensuring Francophone Albertans can access services in French. The secretariat also supports the Francophone community and promotes bilingual service delivery.
services.users: Francophone Albertans, French-speaking newcomers, Francophone organizations

services.main_service_name: Get help with family violence
services.name: Call the family violence information line
services.desc: A 24/7 confidential telephone service providing information, support, and referrals for anyone affected by family violence. Available in more than 170 languages through translation services.
services.users: Anyone affected by family violence, concerned family members, friends

services.main_service_name: Get help with family violence
services.name: Find family violence resources and supports
services.desc: Information about emergency shelters, counselling services, safety planning, protection orders, and other supports available to people experiencing family violence in Alberta.
services.users: People experiencing family violence, service providers, community members

services.main_service_name: Apply for women's scholarships and awards
services.name: Apply for a scholarship or award recognizing women's contributions
services.desc: Alberta offers scholarships and awards that recognize women's achievements and encourage participation in leadership, STEM fields, trades, and public life, including the Persons Case Scholarship and other targeted awards.

services.main_service_name: Find and access a post-secondary program
services.name: Search and compare post-secondary programs
services.desc: Find programs at Alberta's 26 publicly funded institutions: universities, colleges, polytechnics, and specialized arts institutions. Compare programs by institution, credential, and field of study.

services.main_service_name: Find and access a post-secondary program
services.name: Apply to a post-secondary institution
services.desc: Submit applications through ApplyAlberta or directly to institutions. Each institution sets its own admission requirements and deadlines.

services.main_service_name: Find and access a post-secondary program
services.name: Access distance and online learning
services.desc: Many Alberta post-secondary programs offer online, blended, or distance learning options for flexible access regardless of location.

services.main_service_name: Get student financial aid
services.name: Apply for Alberta student loans and grants
services.desc: Apply for needs-based Alberta student loans and grants through a single application. Loans cover tuition, books, and living expenses. Grants don't need to be repaid.

services.main_service_name: Get student financial aid
services.name: Manage your student loan
services.desc: Track your loan balance, view statements, and access tax receipts through MyLoan. Repayment begins six months after you leave school.
services.users: Current and former student loan holders

services.main_service_name: Get student financial aid
services.name: Apply for scholarships and awards
services.desc: Search and apply for Alberta-funded scholarships, awards, and bursaries based on academic achievement, financial need, or specific criteria.

services.main_service_name: Get student financial aid
services.name: Get help repaying your student loan
services.desc: If you're having difficulty repaying, access the Repayment Assistance Plan (RAP) to reduce or pause payments based on your income and family size.

services.main_service_name: Become an apprentice or tradesperson
services.name: Register as an apprentice
services.desc: Register in one of Alberta's designated trades. You'll need an employer willing to sponsor you. Training alternates between on-the-job work and classroom instruction.

relationships.service: Access the Industry Pathways program

services.main_service_name: Become an apprentice or tradesperson
services.name: Write your journeyperson certification exam
services.desc: After completing all apprenticeship periods and technical training, book and write your certification exam to become a certified journeyperson.

services.main_service_name: Become an apprentice or tradesperson
services.name: Get your trade credential recognized
services.desc: If you're an experienced tradesperson from another province or country, apply for credential recognition or challenge the certification exam.

services.main_service_name: Explore career pathways
services.name: Explore careers in skilled trades
services.desc: Learn about 50+ designated trades in Alberta, including wages, job outlook, and training requirements. Skilled Trades Youth Ambassadors visit schools to promote trades careers.
services.users: Students, career changers, parents, school counsellors

services.main_service_name: Explore career pathways
services.name: Access the Industry Pathways program
services.desc: New program developing occupational pathways beyond traditional designated trades, connecting training to employer needs in emerging fields.

relationships.service: Register as an apprentice

services.name: Find information about studying in Alberta
services.desc: Learn about Alberta's post-secondary institutions, programs, costs, and what life is like as an international student in Alberta.

services.goal_name: Economy strengthened by PSIs, trades & regulatory bodies
services.main_service_name: Study in Alberta as an international student
services.name: Access support services as an international student
services.desc: Post-secondary institutions provide dedicated international student services including orientation, academic advising, career support, and mental health resources.

services.goal_name: Economy strengthened by PSIs, trades & regulatory bodies

services.name: Find international study and exchange opportunities
services.desc: Explore exchange programs, study abroad semesters, and international learning opportunities through Alberta's post-secondary institutions and partner institutions worldwide.
services.users: Alberta students interested in international experience

services.main_service_name: Protect crops and livestock with insurance
services.name: Apply for annual crop insurance
services.desc: Protects against crop production losses caused by natural hazards including drought, flooding, hail, frost, excessive moisture, and wildlife damage. Covers annual crops, perennial crops, and honey production.

services.main_service_name: Protect crops and livestock with insurance
services.name: Apply for livestock price insurance
services.desc: Protects cattle and hog producers against drops in market prices. Producers choose a coverage price level and receive an indemnity if the market settlement price falls below it.

services.main_service_name: Protect crops and livestock with insurance
services.name: Apply for hail insurance
services.desc: Straight hail insurance provides coverage for crop losses specifically caused by hail damage. Available as a standalone policy or in combination with multi-peril crop insurance.

services.main_service_name: Protect crops and livestock with insurance
services.name: File a wildlife damage compensation claim
services.desc: Compensation for crop or livestock losses caused by wildlife including waterfowl, elk, deer, bears, and cougars. Administered by AFSC under the wildlife damage compensation program.

services.main_service_name: Stabilize farm income during hardship
services.name: Enrol in income stabilization support
services.desc: Federal-provincial program that provides support when a producer's net income falls significantly below their historical average. Covers income declines from production loss, increased costs, and market conditions.

services.main_service_name: Stabilize farm income during hardship
services.name: Open a government-matched farm savings account
services.desc: Government-matched savings account that helps producers set money aside for small income declines or make investments to mitigate risk. The federal government matches annual deposits up to a specified limit.

services.main_service_name: Stabilize farm income during hardship
services.name: Apply for disaster recovery assistance
services.desc: AgriRecovery provides targeted assistance for extraordinary costs caused by natural disasters such as drought, flooding, or disease outbreaks that go beyond existing programs. Triggered by federal-provincial assessment.

services.main_service_name: Get financing for farm and agribusiness operations
services.name: Apply for a beginning farmer loan
services.desc: Specialized loan products for new and young farmers getting started in agriculture, including the Next Generation Loan and Developing Producer Loan. Lower down payments and flexible terms.
services.users: New farmers, young farmers entering agriculture

services.main_service_name: Get financing for farm and agribusiness operations
services.name: Apply for a farm or agribusiness loan
services.desc: Loans for established agricultural producers and agribusinesses for land purchases, equipment, operating capital, and value-added processing. Includes the Alberta Producer Loan and Revolving Loan products.

services.main_service_name: Keep livestock healthy and traceable
services.name: Report a suspected animal disease
services.desc: Producers and veterinarians must report certain animal diseases within 24 hours. The Chief Provincial Veterinarian coordinates response including quarantine, testing, and control measures to prevent spread.

services.main_service_name: Keep livestock healthy and traceable
services.name: Register your premises for livestock traceability
services.desc: Premises identification is required for all locations where livestock are kept. The system enables rapid response to disease outbreaks by tracking where animals are located.

services.main_service_name: Keep livestock healthy and traceable
services.name: Get guidance on livestock management practices
services.desc: Information on beef production, animal nutrition, handling, welfare, forages and pasture management, and mortality management. Includes publications and extension services delivered through AFSC and regional offices.

services.main_service_name: Ensure food is safe across the supply chain
services.name: Get food safety training and certification
services.desc: Training in Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) and other food safety systems for processors, producers, and food service operators. Includes resources for home-based food preparation businesses.
services.users: Food processors, producers selling direct to consumers

services.main_service_name: Ensure food is safe across the supply chain
services.name: Comply with meat and dairy inspection requirements
services.desc: Provincial inspection of meat processing plants and dairy facilities to ensure safety standards are met. Covers facility licensing, operational standards, and animal welfare at slaughter.
services.users: Meat processors, dairy processors, abattoir operators

services.main_service_name: Manage pests, diseases, and weeds on your land
services.name: Report or get help with an agricultural pest
services.desc: Designated pests including insects, rodents, and plant diseases must be reported. Agricultural fieldmen and service boards assist with identification, control measures, and compliance.

services.main_service_name: Manage pests, diseases, and weeds on your land
services.name: Control noxious and prohibited weeds
services.desc: Landowners are required to control noxious weeds and destroy prohibited noxious weeds on their land. Agricultural fieldmen enforce compliance and provide identification and control guidance.

services.main_service_name: Manage pests, diseases, and weeds on your land
services.name: Get crop disease diagnosis and management advice
services.desc: Information on regulated and common crop diseases, diagnostic services, and integrated pest management strategies. Includes economic threshold data for insect treatment decisions.

services.main_service_name: Access agronomic information and decision tools
services.name: Use weather and climate data for farm planning
services.desc: The Alberta Climate Information Service (ACIS) provides current and historical weather data, forecasts, growing degree days, moisture indices, and other agricultural climate tools for production planning.

services.main_service_name: Access agronomic information and decision tools
services.name: Get soil survey data and land information
services.desc: Access soil survey reports, the Alberta Soil Information Viewer, land capability assessments, and nutrient management planning resources to make informed decisions about cropping and land use.

services.main_service_name: Access agronomic information and decision tools
services.name: Use crop budget and decision tools
services.desc: Online crop budget calculator, cropping alternatives cost-return forecasts, and AgriProfit$ benchmarking data to support production and financial planning decisions.

services.main_service_name: Develop markets and grow agricultural trade
services.name: Access market information and price reports
services.desc: Weekly livestock and crop market reviews, Alberta crop reports, agricultural statistics, and economic indicators to support marketing decisions and industry planning.

services.main_service_name: Develop markets and grow agricultural trade
services.name: Get support for agricultural trade and export
services.desc: Services to help agribusinesses access international markets, connect with buyers and investors, and navigate export requirements. Includes trade missions and matchmaking services.

services.main_service_name: Develop markets and grow agricultural trade
services.name: Apply for agricultural sustainability funding
services.desc: The Sustainable Canadian Agricultural Partnership provides cost-shared grants for eligible projects in environmental sustainability, market development, science and research, and public trust. Five-year federal-provincial-territorial framework.

services.main_service_name: Access irrigation and water management services
services.name: Connect with your irrigation district
services.desc: Alberta's 13 irrigation districts manage water delivery infrastructure serving over 1.5 million acres of irrigated farmland. Producers within a district access water allocation, infrastructure expansion, and water-sharing arrangements.

services.main_service_name: Access irrigation and water management services
services.name: Get farm water testing and quality information
services.desc: Testing services for farm drinking water, livestock water, and irrigation water quality. Helps producers identify contamination risks and take corrective action.
