services.desc: Legal document naming someone to make decisions if you become incapacitated. Register it with the Personal Directives Registry for safekeeping.

services.goal_name: Albertans have access to quality continuing care close to home
services.main_service_name: Protect your rights and plan for the future
services.name: Get decision-making support for a vulnerable person
services.desc: Court process to get decision-making authority for vulnerable adults
services.users: Families of adults who need decision-making support

services.goal_name: Albertans have access to quality continuing care close to home
services.main_service_name: Protect your rights and plan for the future
services.name: Request a capacity assessment
services.desc: Medical evaluation of a person's decision-making ability

services.goal_name: Albertans have access to quality continuing care close to home
services.main_service_name: Protect your rights and plan for the future
services.name: File a complaint about a guardian or trustee
services.desc: Report concerns about abuse or mismanagement of affairs

services.goal_name: Albertans have access to quality continuing care close to home
services.main_service_name: Protect your rights and plan for the future
services.name: Get help with legal and financial matters for a vulnerable person
services.desc: Guardianship and trusteeship services for vulnerable adults without private trustees

services.main_service_name: Get financial guidance and affordability advice
services.name: Access financial literacy resources and online courses
services.desc: Free financial literacy tools, guides, and online courses covering budgeting, debt management, savings strategies, credit building, and planning for life events such as home buying, family planning, job loss, and retirement.
services.users: Students, working families, seniors, anyone managing personal finances

services.main_service_name: Get financial guidance and affordability advice
services.name: Find ways to reduce everyday costs
services.desc: Practical guidance on reducing costs for food, housing, utilities, transportation, and childcare. Includes tips for lowering energy bills, accessing government benefits, and finding community supports.

services.main_service_name: Apply for fuel and heating cost relief
services.name: Apply for the farm fuel tax exemption
services.desc: Eligible agricultural producers can apply for a 9-cent-per-litre provincial fuel tax exemption on marked (dyed) gasoline and diesel fuel used for farming operations, reducing input costs for Alberta's agricultural sector.

services.main_service_name: Apply for fuel and heating cost relief
services.name: Apply for the remote area heating allowance
services.desc: Albertans in areas without access to natural gas can apply for a rebate of up to 25% on heating oil and propane costs, helping offset higher heating expenses in rural and remote communities.
services.users: Rural households without natural gas access

services.main_service_name: Understand and compare energy providers
services.name: Compare electricity retailers and plans
services.desc: Alberta's deregulated electricity market allows consumers to choose their electricity retailer. The Utilities Consumer Advocate provides tools to compare rates, contract terms, and retailer performance to help consumers find the best plan.

relationships.service: Develop utility market policy and regulation

services.main_service_name: Understand and compare energy providers
services.name: Compare natural gas retailers and plans
services.desc: Alberta consumers can choose their natural gas retailer. The Utilities Consumer Advocate provides rate comparison tools and information about contract options to help consumers make informed choices about their gas supply.

relationships.service: Develop utility market policy and regulation

services.main_service_name: Understand and compare energy providers
services.name: Learn about micro-generation and renewable energy options
services.desc: Albertans who generate their own electricity from renewable sources (solar, wind, micro-hydro) can connect to the grid and receive credit for excess power. Information about eligibility, equipment, and the application process is available.
services.users: Homeowners, businesses, farms considering renewable energy

services.main_service_name: Get help with a utility problem
services.name: Get help resolving a utility dispute
services.desc: The Utilities Consumer Advocate provides free, independent assistance to residential, farm, and small business consumers with utility billing disputes, retailer complaints, and questions about their energy services. Interpreter services are available.
services.users: Residential consumers, farm consumers, small businesses

relationships.service: Develop utility market policy and regulation

services.main_service_name: Get help with a utility problem
services.name: Get help preventing utility disconnection
services.desc: Consumers at risk of having their electricity or natural gas disconnected can contact the Utilities Consumer Advocate for help understanding their rights, negotiating payment arrangements, and accessing financial assistance programs.

services.main_service_name: Access rural utility services
services.name: Get information about rural electrification
services.desc: Alberta supports 32 Rural Electrification Associations that deliver electricity to rural areas. The Rural Electric Program provides grants to help farmers and rural residents access electricity at reasonable cost.
services.users: Rural residents, farmers, Rural Electrification Associations

services.main_service_name: Access rural utility services
services.name: Get information about rural natural gas service
services.desc: Alberta oversees 85 rural natural gas utilities serving more than 380,000 consumers. The ministry ensures safe design, construction, and operation of rural gas distribution systems.
services.users: Rural residents, gas co-op members, rural gas utilities

services.main_service_name: Access rural utility services
services.name: Get information about rural water co-operatives
services.desc: Alberta supports more than 170 water co-operatives that provide treated water to rural communities. The ministry provides technical and governance support to ensure safe and reliable water service.
services.users: Rural residents, water co-op members, water co-operative boards

services.goal_name: Services and supports are accessible and responsive
services.main_service_name: Report and respond to child safety concerns
services.name: Report concerns about child abuse or neglect
services.desc: Anyone who suspects a child is being abused or neglected is legally required to report it. The 24/7 hotline connects you to trained intake workers.
services.users: Anyone concerned about a child's safety

services.goal_name: Services and supports are accessible and responsive
services.main_service_name: Report and respond to child safety concerns
services.name: Get family preservation support
services.desc: When children can safely remain at home, caseworkers provide family enhancement agreements, connecting families with counselling, parenting support, and community resources.

services.goal_name: Services and supports are accessible and responsive
services.main_service_name: Report and respond to child safety concerns
services.name: Get connected to local family support services
services.desc: Community-based Family Resource Networks provide prevention and early intervention support including parenting programs, home visitation, and family counselling — before child intervention is needed.

services.goal_name: Services and supports are accessible and responsive
services.main_service_name: Get support with family violence
services.name: Get help if you're experiencing family violence
services.desc: Access emergency shelters, counselling, safety planning, and community-based supports. Alberta's family violence information line connects you to local resources.
services.users: Individuals and families experiencing family violence

services.goal_name: Services and supports are accessible and responsive
services.main_service_name: Get support with family violence
services.name: Get support after sexual violence
services.desc: Connect with sexual assault centres, counselling, and community supports across Alberta.

services.goal_name: Services and supports are accessible and responsive
services.main_service_name: Support youth aging out of care
services.name: Get housing and life skills support after leaving care
services.desc: Young adults aged 18-22 who were in care receive financial support, life skills coaching, mentorship, and help with housing, education, and employment.

services.goal_name: Services and supports are accessible and responsive
services.main_service_name: Support youth aging out of care
services.name: Get funding for post-secondary education after care
services.desc: Former youth in care aged 22-31 can access financial support to pursue post-secondary education or upgrading, with living allowances and tuition coverage.
services.users: Former youth in care pursuing education (22-31)

services.goal_name: Vulnerable children, youth and individuals are safe and supported
services.main_service_name: Provide care for children who need it
services.name: Become a foster caregiver
services.desc: Apply to become a foster caregiver to provide temporary, safe homes for children who can't stay with their families. Training and ongoing support are provided.

services.goal_name: Vulnerable children, youth and individuals are safe and supported
services.main_service_name: Provide care for children who need it
services.name: Arrange kinship care for a family member's child
services.desc: If a child in your extended family or community can't stay with their parents, kinship care lets them stay with someone they know and trust. Financial support and training are available.
services.users: Extended family members and close community members

services.goal_name: Vulnerable children, youth and individuals are safe and supported
services.main_service_name: Provide care for children who need it
services.name: Get support as a foster or kinship caregiver
services.desc: Caregivers receive skill fees, support payments, training, respite care, and access to mental health supports. Caregiver support workers help with challenges.

services.goal_name: Vulnerable children, youth and individuals are safe and supported
services.main_service_name: Find a permanent home for a child
services.name: Adopt a child in government care
services.desc: Children who cannot return to their birth families need permanent adoptive homes. The ministry matches children with prospective adoptive families and provides post-adoption support.

services.goal_name: Vulnerable children, youth and individuals are safe and supported
services.main_service_name: Find a permanent home for a child
services.name: Pursue a private or international adoption
services.desc: For private domestic or international adoptions, licensed adoption agencies facilitate the process. The ministry provides oversight and post-placement support.

services.goal_name: Vulnerable children, youth and individuals are safe and supported
services.main_service_name: Find a permanent home for a child
services.name: Get post-adoption support
services.desc: Adoptive families can access support services including counselling, respite, and financial assistance after an adoption is finalized.

services.main_service_name: Get government news and announcements
services.name: Read government news releases and announcements
services.desc: The Government of Alberta publishes news releases, statements, and announcements on alberta.ca/news. Citizens can browse by ministry, topic, or date to stay informed about government decisions, policy changes, funding announcements, and program updates.

services.main_service_name: Get government news and announcements
services.name: Subscribe to government news and topic alerts
services.desc: Albertans can subscribe to email alerts for government news filtered by ministry, topic, or region. Subscribers receive notifications when new releases, announcements, or policy updates are published.
services.users: Citizens, media, stakeholders wanting proactive updates

services.main_service_name: Get government news and announcements
services.name: Receive emergency alerts and critical safety information
services.desc: The Government of Alberta issues emergency alerts for wildfires, severe weather, public safety threats, and other critical events through the Alberta Emergency Alert system. Alerts are distributed through mobile devices, broadcast media, and online channels.

services.main_service_name: Find government information and services online
services.name: Search for government services and information
services.desc: Alberta.ca is the Government of Alberta's central website, providing access to all ministry services, programs, forms, and information. CPE manages the platform's content standards, information architecture, search functionality, and accessibility.
services.users: All Albertans seeking government services or information

services.main_service_name: Find government information and services online
services.name: Access government publications, reports, and data
services.desc: The Open Government portal and alberta.ca provide access to government publications including annual reports, business plans, budget documents, datasets, and research reports across all ministries.

services.main_service_name: Participate in a public consultation or engagement
services.name: Find and join an open public consultation
services.desc: Albertans can search for open public consultations and engagement opportunities across all government ministries. Topics range from policy development and legislative changes to infrastructure projects and program design. Each engagement explains the issue, timeline, and how to participate.
services.users: All Albertans interested in contributing to government decisions

services.main_service_name: Participate in a public consultation or engagement
services.name: Submit feedback on a proposed policy, regulation, or project
services.desc: Albertans can submit written feedback, complete surveys, or participate in online discussions on proposed policies, regulations, and projects. Submissions are reviewed and considered in the decision-making process, with summary reports published afterward.
services.users: Citizens, stakeholders, industry, organizations with views on government proposals

services.main_service_name: Participate in a public consultation or engagement
services.name: View results and outcomes of past public engagements
services.desc: After a public engagement closes, the government publishes summary reports showing what was heard, how many people participated, and how public input influenced the final decision. This closes the feedback loop and builds public trust.
services.users: Past participants, researchers, media, general public

services.main_service_name: Contact the government or your elected representative
services.name: Send a message to the Premier or a minister
services.desc: Albertans can write directly to the Premier or any cabinet minister to share views, raise concerns, or request information. Contact forms, mailing addresses, and email contacts are published for all ministers.

services.main_service_name: Contact the government or your elected representative
services.name: Find contact information for a government office or service
services.desc: Alberta.ca provides a directory of government offices, ministry contacts, and service-specific phone numbers and email addresses. Albertans can find the right contact for any government program or inquiry.
services.users: All Albertans needing to reach a specific government office

services.main_service_name: Request access to government records
services.name: Submit a freedom of information request
services.desc: Any person can submit a formal access request under the FOIP Act to obtain records held by a Government of Alberta public body. Requests are processed within legislated timelines, with fees for search and copying. Certain records may be withheld under specific exemptions.

services.main_service_name: Request access to government records
services.name: Appeal a decision on an access request
services.desc: If a FOIP request is denied, partially granted, or if fees seem unreasonable, the requester can ask the Information and Privacy Commissioner to review the decision. The Commissioner can order disclosure or recommend changes.
services.users: FOIP requesters dissatisfied with a public body's decision

services.main_service_name: Protect your personal information held by government
services.name: Request your own personal information from a public body
services.desc: Albertans have the right under FOIP to access their own personal information held by government public bodies. This includes records related to benefits, licensing, enforcement actions, and any other files where the individual's information appears.
services.users: Individuals seeking their own records from government

services.main_service_name: Protect your personal information held by government
services.name: File a privacy complaint about how government handled your information
services.desc: If an Albertan believes a public body has improperly collected, used, or disclosed their personal information, they can file a complaint with the Information and Privacy Commissioner. The Commissioner investigates and can order corrective action.
services.users: Individuals concerned about government handling of their personal information

services.main_service_name: Protect your personal information held by government
services.name: Learn about your privacy rights and how government uses personal information
services.desc: The government publishes privacy impact assessments, collection notices, and plain-language guides explaining how personal information is collected, used, and protected by public bodies under FOIP. Albertans can learn their rights and what to expect.
services.users: All Albertans, privacy advocates, legal professionals

services.main_service_name: Choose how your child learns
services.name: Find curriculum resources and programs of study
services.desc: Find Alberta's K to 12 curriculum, including the new curriculum being implemented across subjects and grades. Resources for teachers, parents, and students.

services.main_service_name: Choose how your child learns
services.name: Register for home education
services.desc: Register your child for home education through your local school board or a willing school board. Home education must align with Alberta curriculum outcomes.
services.users: Parents choosing to educate at home

services.main_service_name: Choose how your child learns
services.name: Enrol in distance or online learning (K-12)
services.desc: Enrol your child in accredited online or blended learning programs for K to 12. Available through school authorities that offer distance education.
services.users: Parents and students needing flexible learning

services.main_service_name: Take provincial exams and tests
services.name: Prepare for and write Provincial Achievement Tests
services.desc: Provincial Achievement Tests (PATs) are administered in Grades 6 and 9 in core subjects. Results help measure student learning and inform school improvement.
services.users: Grade 6 and 9 students and their parents

services.main_service_name: Take provincial exams and tests
services.name: Prepare for and write diploma exams
services.desc: Diploma examinations are written in Grade 12 in core academic subjects and count for 30% of the final course mark.

services.main_service_name: Take provincial exams and tests
services.name: Check assessment results and school performance data
services.desc: View school and jurisdiction-level results for PATs and diploma exams. Accountability pillar results help parents compare school performance.

services.main_service_name: Enrol and support your child in school
services.name: Enrol your child in school
services.desc: Contact your local school or school authority to register your child for ECS (kindergarten) through Grade 12. Schools accept registrations year-round.

services.main_service_name: Enrol and support your child in school
services.name: Get specialized learning support for your child
services.desc: Students with identified learning needs can access specialized supports, programming, and individualized program plans (IPPs) through their school.
services.users: Parents of children with learning needs

services.main_service_name: Enrol and support your child in school
services.name: Arrange student transportation
services.desc: School authorities provide transportation based on distance: 1.6 km for ECS-Grade 6, 2 km for Grades 7-12. Contact your school authority for routes and eligibility.
services.users: Parents needing transportation for their children

services.main_service_name: Enrol and support your child in school
services.name: Get school mental health support
services.desc: Schools provide counselling, mental health literacy programs, and connections to community resources for students experiencing mental health challenges.

services.main_service_name: Enrol and support your child in school
services.name: Join your school council
services.desc: Every school has a school council where parents can be involved in school decisions. Each council receives a $500 annual engagement grant.

services.main_service_name: Explore careers and earn workplace credentials
services.name: Explore career and technology studies (CTS)
services.desc: Hands-on learning in areas like information technology, health, trades, media, and business. Available from Grade 7 through 12.

services.main_service_name: Explore careers and earn workplace credentials
services.name: Earn dual credit (high school + post-secondary)
services.desc: Earn both high school and post-secondary credits simultaneously. The Provincial Dual Credit Strategy connects Grade 10-12 students with college and polytechnic courses.
services.users: High school students interested in post-secondary

services.main_service_name: Explore careers and earn workplace credentials
services.name: Earn credits through work experience or RAP
services.desc: Earn high school credits through work experience, the Registered Apprenticeship Program (RAP), or green certificate programs. Connects classroom learning to real workplaces.

relationships.service: Learn about minimum wage, hours of work, overtime, and leave entitlements

services.goal_name: K-12 education system and workforce are well-managed
services.main_service_name: Get certified as a teacher
services.name: Apply for teacher certification
services.desc: Apply for an Alberta teaching certificate after completing an approved teacher preparation program. Certification is required to teach in Alberta's K-12 system.

relationships.service: Transfer teaching credentials from another jurisdiction

services.goal_name: K-12 education system and workforce are well-managed
services.main_service_name: Get certified as a teacher
services.name: Transfer teaching credentials from another jurisdiction
services.desc: Teachers certified in another Canadian province or internationally can apply for Alberta certification with their existing credentials and teaching experience.

relationships.service: Apply for teacher certification

services.goal_name: Families have access to quality, inclusive, affordable childcare
services.main_service_name: Find and afford child care
services.name: Search for licensed child care
services.desc: Find licensed child care programs near you including daycare, out-of-school care, and family day homes. Alberta is working toward an average $10/day fee.

relationships.service: License and monitor child care programs

services.goal_name: Families have access to quality, inclusive, affordable childcare
services.main_service_name: Find and afford child care
services.name: Apply for child care subsidies
services.desc: Low- and middle-income families can apply for subsidies to reduce child care costs. Eligibility is based on household income and family size.
services.users: Families needing help with child care costs

relationships.service: License and monitor child care programs

services.goal_name: Families have access to quality, inclusive, affordable childcare
services.main_service_name: Find and afford child care
services.name: Find inclusive child care for a child with extra needs
services.desc: The Provincial Inclusive Child Care Program supports children with extra support needs to participate fully in licensed child care programs.
services.users: Parents of children with additional needs

relationships.service: License and monitor child care programs

services.goal_name: Families have access to quality, inclusive, affordable childcare
services.main_service_name: Get your child assessed and supported early
services.name: Get early intervention for your child
services.desc: The Early Childhood Intervention Program provides developmental support for children birth to age 6 who have disabilities or developmental delays.
services.users: Parents of young children with developmental concerns

services.main_service_name: Acquire petroleum and natural gas rights
services.name: Bid on petroleum and natural gas rights at public sale
services.desc: Alberta holds regular public offerings of Crown petroleum and natural gas rights through a competitive bidding process. Companies submit sealed bids on posted parcels; highest qualified bid wins the lease. Sales are held on set schedules throughout the year.
services.users: Oil and gas exploration companies, producers

services.main_service_name: Acquire petroleum and natural gas rights
services.name: Request a direct purchase of petroleum and natural gas rights
services.desc: Companies may request to purchase specific Crown petroleum and natural gas rights outside the regular public sale process through a direct purchase request (posting request). The department evaluates and may post the requested parcel at a subsequent sale.
services.users: Oil and gas companies seeking specific parcels

services.main_service_name: Acquire petroleum and natural gas rights
services.name: Transfer or maintain your petroleum and natural gas agreement
services.desc: Holders of Crown petroleum and natural gas agreements can transfer, continue, or group their leases. The Electronic Transfer System (ETS) supports agreement transfers, continuations, amalgamations, and amendments.
services.users: Petroleum and natural gas agreement holders

services.main_service_name: Acquire oil sands, coal, and mineral rights
services.name: Bid on oil sands rights at public sale
services.desc: The ministry holds competitive public offerings of Crown oil sands rights. Companies submit bids on posted parcels in Alberta's three major oil sands deposits (Athabasca, Cold Lake, Peace River). Results are published after each sale.
services.users: Oil sands producers and exploration companies

services.main_service_name: Acquire oil sands, coal, and mineral rights
services.name: Acquire coal leases and maintain coal agreements
services.desc: Companies can acquire Crown coal rights through direct purchase or posting request, and maintain existing coal leases. The department administers coal leasing on Crown lands and tracks lease maintenance, payments, and renewals.

services.main_service_name: Acquire oil sands, coal, and mineral rights
services.name: Apply for metallic and industrial mineral permits and leases
services.desc: Companies can apply for permits to explore for metallic and industrial minerals, and convert permits to leases for production. Covers lithium, potash, limestone, salt, gemstones, and other non-energy minerals. Includes brine-hosted mineral public offerings.

services.main_service_name: Report and pay royalties on energy and mineral production
services.name: Report and pay conventional oil and gas royalties
services.desc: Producers report monthly production and calculate royalties owing on conventional crude oil, natural gas, and natural gas liquids extracted from Crown mineral rights. The Crown Royalty Information System processes returns and payments.

services.main_service_name: Report and pay royalties on energy and mineral production
services.name: Report and pay oil sands royalties
services.desc: Oil sands operators report production and costs under the Oil Sands Royalty Regime. Pre-payout projects pay a lower gross royalty rate; post-payout projects pay the greater of gross or net royalty. The ministry audits returns and manages project-specific Crown agreements.

services.main_service_name: Report and pay royalties on energy and mineral production
services.name: Report and pay coal and mineral royalties
services.desc: Coal producers and mineral lessees report production volumes and pay royalties to the Crown. Coal royalties are based on mine-mouth value; mineral royalties vary by commodity. Reporting forms and schedules are available online.

services.main_service_name: Access energy market and industry data
services.name: Access oil and gas production and reserves data
services.desc: Publicly available data on Alberta's oil, gas, and oil sands production volumes, reserves, well counts, and historical trends. Includes monthly production reports, reserve estimates, and statistical bulletins published by the ministry and the Alberta Energy Regulator.

relationships.service: Design and maintain royalty and fiscal frameworks for energy and mineral resources

services.main_service_name: Access energy market and industry data
services.name: Purchase mineral and surface rights mapping and information
services.desc: Payment, maps and information services provides mineral and surface rights mapping, energy information letters, subscription services, and other online resources. Mineral ownership data helps companies, lawyers, and landowners verify who holds rights.
services.users: Land agents, lawyers, oil and gas companies, landowners

relationships.service: Design and maintain royalty and fiscal frameworks for energy and mineral resources

services.main_service_name: Access energy market and industry data
services.name: Search metallic and industrial mineral assessment reports
services.desc: Companies and researchers can search publicly available mineral assessment reports submitted by permit holders. Reports include geological surveys, drilling results, and resource assessments for Alberta's non-energy mineral deposits.

services.main_service_name: Understand royalty obligations and freehold mineral tax
services.name: Access royalty rate and program information
services.desc: Information on Alberta's royalty rates, incentive programs, and calculation methodologies for conventional oil and gas, oil sands, coal, and minerals. Includes rate schedules, royalty reduction programs, and reference prices.

services.main_service_name: Understand royalty obligations and freehold mineral tax
services.name: Pay freehold mineral rights tax
services.desc: Owners of freehold mineral rights that produce oil or gas pay an annual tax based on production value. The ministry issues assessments, collects payments, and handles appeals. Approximately 18% of Alberta's mineral rights are freehold.

services.main_service_name: Engage with hydrogen and clean energy opportunities
services.name: Learn about Alberta's hydrogen roadmap and investment opportunities
services.desc: Information on Alberta's hydrogen strategy including five target markets (gas utilities, transportation, industrial, power generation, exports), seven policy pillars, and investment opportunities. Alberta is the largest hydrogen producer in Canada.
services.users: Clean energy companies, investors, researchers, municipalities

services.main_service_name: Engage with hydrogen and clean energy opportunities
services.name: Learn about carbon capture, utilization, and storage opportunities
services.desc: Information on Alberta's CCUS framework, including pore space tenure, storage hub development, and regulatory requirements for companies seeking to develop carbon storage projects. Alberta has decades of CCUS experience and extensive geological storage capacity.

services.main_service_name: Participate in critical minerals and coal modernization
services.name: Explore lithium and critical mineral development opportunities
services.desc: Information on Alberta's critical mineral potential including lithium extracted from oilfield brines, potash, rare earth elements, and helium. The Alberta Geological Survey confirms Alberta has one of the world's largest lithium resources. Brine-hosted mineral public offerings available.
services.users: Mining companies, clean technology manufacturers, investors

services.main_service_name: Participate in critical minerals and coal modernization
services.name: Learn about coal industry modernization
services.desc: Information on the Coal Industry Modernization Initiative (CIMI), which establishes a modern legislative and regulatory framework for responsible coal mining in Alberta. Covers transition from thermal to metallurgical coal, environmental protection, and community engagement.
services.users: Coal companies, communities near coal operations, environmental groups

services.main_service_name: Participate in critical minerals and coal modernization
services.name: Access information on methane reduction and emissions management
services.desc: Alberta's made-in-Alberta approach to methane reduction in oil and gas operations. Information on the provincial regulatory framework, emission reduction targets, industry reporting requirements, and technology solutions for reducing methane emissions.
services.users: Oil and gas producers, environmental consultants

services.main_service_name: Monitor air quality and protect your health
services.name: Check the Air Quality Health Index for your area
services.desc: The Air Quality Health Index (AQHI) provides real-time health risk ratings from 1 (low) to 10+ (very high) for communities across Alberta. Helps residents, especially those with respiratory conditions, plan outdoor activities safely.
services.users: All Albertans, people with asthma or respiratory conditions, outdoor workers

services.main_service_name: Monitor air quality and protect your health
services.name: View air quality advisories and wildfire smoke information
services.desc: Air quality advisories are issued when conditions may pose health risks, particularly during wildfire season. Includes smoke forecasts, particulate matter readings, and protective action recommendations.
services.users: All Albertans, communities affected by wildfire smoke

services.main_service_name: Monitor air quality and protect your health
services.name: Access air quality data and monitoring reports
services.desc: Historical and current air quality data from monitoring stations across Alberta. Includes ambient monitoring data, air quality reports, and information from the Alberta Environmental Science Program and airshed organizations.

relationships.service: Administer environmental assessment, approval, and compliance frameworks

services.main_service_name: Report and reduce industrial emissions
services.name: Report greenhouse gas emissions under the regulatory framework
services.desc: Large industrial emitters (facilities emitting 100,000+ tonnes CO₂e/yr) must report emissions and comply with Alberta's emissions reduction requirements. The framework sets benchmarks and requires facilities to reduce emissions intensity or pay into the compliance fund.
services.users: Large industrial facilities, oil sands operators, power generators

services.main_service_name: Report and reduce industrial emissions
services.name: Purchase or sell emissions offset credits and performance credits
services.desc: Alberta's carbon market allows facilities to use emissions offsets, emission performance credits, and fund credits to meet compliance obligations. Offset project developers can register projects and sell verified offsets.
services.users: Offset project developers, regulated facilities, carbon market participants

services.main_service_name: Get water licences, approvals, and well information
services.name: Apply for a water licence or approval
services.desc: Anyone diverting water from a river, lake, or groundwater source (beyond household use) needs a water licence. Approvals are required for activities affecting water bodies such as constructing dams, bridges, or shoreline works. Applications are reviewed against available allocation and environmental impact.

relationships.service: Administer environmental assessment, approval, and compliance frameworks

services.main_service_name: Get water licences, approvals, and well information
services.name: Look up groundwater information and water well records
services.desc: Alberta's water well database contains records for hundreds of thousands of water wells. Landowners, drillers, and consultants can search for well logs, aquifer data, and groundwater quality information.
services.users: Landowners, well drillers, environmental consultants, municipalities

services.main_service_name: Get water licences, approvals, and well information
services.name: Access water quality data and river condition reports
services.desc: Water quality monitoring data for rivers, lakes, and groundwater across Alberta. Includes the Alberta River Basins website for current conditions, water quality indices, and long-term trend reports.
services.users: Researchers, municipalities, water treatment operators, recreation users

relationships.service: Administer environmental assessment, approval, and compliance frameworks

services.main_service_name: Prepare for and respond to flood and drought
services.name: Check flood maps and river advisories
services.desc: Flood hazard maps, river flow forecasts, and flood advisories help communities prepare for high water events. The Alberta Rivers website provides real-time data on river levels and flow rates across the province.
services.users: Homeowners in floodplains, municipalities, emergency managers

services.main_service_name: Prepare for and respond to flood and drought
services.name: Access drought information and water conservation guidance
services.desc: Drought condition reports, water shortage advisories, and guidance on water conservation for households, agriculture, and municipalities. Alberta is at risk of droughts and water shortages, especially in southern regions.
services.users: Farmers, municipalities, all Albertans during drought conditions

services.main_service_name: Prepare for and respond to flood and drought
services.name: Apply for water and wastewater infrastructure grants
services.desc: Financial assistance programs for municipalities and First Nations to build or upgrade drinking water and wastewater treatment systems. Includes the Water for Life strategy capital funding and federal-provincial cost-shared programs.
services.users: Municipalities, First Nations, regional services commissions

services.main_service_name: Get environmental approvals for projects and activities
services.name: Apply for an environmental approval or registration
services.desc: Industrial activities such as manufacturing, waste processing, oil sands operations, and large-scale development require environmental approvals under EPEA. Registrations cover smaller-scale activities under codes of practice. Applications are assessed for environmental impact.
services.users: Industrial operators, developers, waste facility operators

services.main_service_name: Get environmental approvals for projects and activities
services.name: Report a release of a substance to the environment
services.desc: Anyone who releases or discovers a release of a substance that may cause an adverse effect on the environment must report it immediately. The 24-hour environmental emergency line accepts reports of spills, releases, and environmental incidents.
services.users: Industrial operators, landowners, any Albertan who witnesses a spill

services.main_service_name: Manage waste, recycling, and hazardous materials
services.name: Comply with hazardous waste management requirements
services.desc: Generators, carriers, and receivers of hazardous waste must follow tracking, storage, treatment, and disposal requirements. Includes registration, manifesting shipments, and reporting to ensure hazardous materials are handled safely.
services.users: Hazardous waste generators, carriers, treatment facilities

services.main_service_name: Manage waste, recycling, and hazardous materials
services.name: Get certified as a landfill or composting facility operator
services.desc: Operators of regulated landfill and composting facilities in Alberta must hold valid certification. The ministry sets training requirements, administers exams, and issues operator certificates.
services.users: Landfill and composting facility operators, municipalities

services.main_service_name: Manage waste, recycling, and hazardous materials
services.name: Learn about recycling programs and extended producer responsibility
services.desc: Alberta's extended producer responsibility (EPR) system shifts the cost of collecting, sorting, and recycling consumer products and packaging from municipalities to producers. Information on recycling programs, accepted materials, and how producers comply.
services.users: Consumers, municipalities, product producers and retailers

services.main_service_name: Protect wildlife and species at risk
services.name: Look up the status of a wild species in Alberta
services.desc: Search the provincial species status database to find whether a fish, wildlife, or plant species is designated as at risk, may be at risk, sensitive, or secure. The Endangered Species Conservation Committee assesses detailed status.

services.main_service_name: Protect wildlife and species at risk
services.name: Report a wildlife concern or human-wildlife interaction
services.desc: Albertans can report injured wildlife, problem wildlife, poaching, and human-wildlife conflicts. Contact information varies by situation — Report a Poacher line, Fish and Wildlife enforcement, or regional offices.
services.users: All Albertans, rural landowners, outdoor recreationists

services.main_service_name: Protect wildlife and species at risk
services.name: Access environmental science data and state of the environment reporting
services.desc: Alberta's Environmental Science Program and the Office of the Chief Scientist provide data, reports, and indicators on the condition of air, water, land, and biodiversity across the province. Supports evidence-based environmental decision-making.
services.users: Researchers, environmental consultants, policy analysts, educators

services.main_service_name: Manage Alberta's forests sustainably
services.name: Access forest management standards and guidelines
services.desc: Plans, guidelines, and standards for sustainable forest management in Alberta, including the Forest Management Planning Standard.
services.users: Forest industry operators, professionals, and researchers

relationships.service: Access wildfire maps and data

services.main_service_name: Manage Alberta's forests sustainably
services.name: Find a regulated forestry professional
services.desc: Professionals regulated under the Regulated Forest Management Profession Act work with government and industry to apply sound forestry principles in Alberta.
services.users: Forest industry, landowners, and the public

services.main_service_name: Manage Alberta's forests sustainably
services.name: Get forestry and wildfire training
services.desc: Technical and safety training programs for forest and wildfire management, currently delivered at the Hinton Training Centre.
services.users: Forest and wildfire workers, industry partners, First Nations communities

services.main_service_name: Allocate timber harvesting rights
services.name: Apply for a timber quota
services.desc: Long-term timber harvesting tenure granted under the Forests Act. Timber quotas are one of Alberta's three forest tenure systems.
services.users: Forest companies and commercial timber operators

services.main_service_name: Allocate timber harvesting rights
services.name: Apply for a timber permit
services.desc: Shorter-term commercial timber harvesting authorizations granted under the Forests Act.
services.users: Forest companies, small operators, and contractors

services.main_service_name: Allocate timber harvesting rights
services.name: Pay timber dues and Crown fees
services.desc: Fees paid to the Crown for timber harvested under quotas, permits, and forest management agreements.

services.main_service_name: Allocate timber harvesting rights
services.name: Report timber damage assessment
services.desc: Damage assessment process for timber lost to fire, insect, or other causes, used to adjust Crown fees and harvesting plans.

services.main_service_name: Cut trees for personal use
services.name: Get a personal-use tree cutting permit
services.desc: Non-commercial permits for firewood collection, transplants, and Christmas trees on Crown land, including area maps and rules.
services.users: Alberta residents collecting firewood or trees for personal use

services.main_service_name: Operate a forestry business
services.name: Access forest business, market, and trade information
services.desc: Information on Alberta's forest products sector, markets, and trade access.

services.main_service_name: Operate a forestry business
services.name: Access forestry directives and standard operating procedures
services.desc: Policy directives and SOPs that provide formal direction for Alberta's forest policy and legislation.

services.main_service_name: Visit a provincial park
services.name: Plan a visit to a provincial park
services.desc: Provincial park locations, rules, events, maps, and planning resources across Alberta's parks and protected areas network.

services.main_service_name: Visit a provincial park
services.name: Reserve a campsite
services.desc: Reserve campsites, group camping areas, and comfort camping units in Alberta's provincial parks — online or by phone.

services.main_service_name: Visit a provincial park
services.name: Buy a park conservation pass
services.desc: Daily or yearly pass required in designated conservation areas. Currently covers Kananaskis Country and the Bow Valley Corridor; additional areas may be added. Revenue supports conservation.
services.users: Visitors to designated park conservation areas

services.main_service_name: Visit a provincial park
services.name: Check park advisories and closures
services.desc: Current advisories, fire bans, public safety notices, and closures in provincial parks.

services.main_service_name: Camp and explore public land
services.name: Get a Public Lands Camping Pass
services.desc: Required for anyone 18 and older who random camps along the Eastern Slopes of the Rocky Mountains.
services.users: Alberta residents and visitors who random camp on Crown land

services.main_service_name: Camp and explore public land
services.name: Find a public land recreation area or trail
services.desc: Designated public land recreation areas, trails, and day-use sites across Alberta's Crown land.

services.main_service_name: Camp and explore public land
services.name: Check Public Land Use Zone rules
services.desc: Permitted activities and regulations for recreational use within Public Land Use Zones (PLUZs).

services.main_service_name: Camp and explore public land
services.name: Check public land closures and advisories
services.desc: Current trail, area, and road closures on Crown land.

services.main_service_name: Camp and explore public land
services.name: Access outdoor recreation maps and guides
services.desc: Downloadable maps, fact sheets, and guides to help plan recreation activities on Alberta's public land.

services.main_service_name: Camp and explore public land
services.name: Search Crown land enforcement history
services.desc: Look up enforcement records taken against a company or individual by the Lands Division. Shared delivery with Environment and Protected Areas.

services.main_service_name: Help prevent wildfires
services.name: Get a fire permit
services.desc: Required for any burning in the Forest Protection Area from March 1 to October 31, with the exception of a campfire.
services.users: Residents, industry, and contractors burning in the Forest Protection Area

relationships.service: Check current fire bans and restrictions

services.main_service_name: Help prevent wildfires
services.name: Check current fire bans and restrictions
services.desc: Live list of fire advisories, restrictions, and bans across Alberta's Forest Protection Area and parks.

relationships.service: Get a fire permit

services.main_service_name: Help prevent wildfires
services.name: Practise safe burning
services.desc: Guidance on burning debris, agricultural burning, and other controlled burns to reduce wildfire risk.

services.main_service_name: Help prevent wildfires
services.name: Prepare your property for wildfire (FireSmart)
services.desc: FireSmart Alberta community and homeowner program to reduce wildfire risk to homes, neighbourhoods, and critical infrastructure.

services.main_service_name: Respond to wildfires
services.name: Report a wildfire
services.desc: Report a wildfire in Alberta's Forest Protection Area by calling 310-FIRE (310-3473) or using the online form.

services.main_service_name: Respond to wildfires
services.name: Check wildfire status
services.desc: Live dashboard of current wildfire locations, sizes, status, and response in Alberta.
services.users: Alberta residents, visitors, municipalities, and media

services.main_service_name: Respond to wildfires
services.name: Access wildfire maps and data
services.desc: Wildfire maps, historical data, and GIS resources for research, planning, and operational use.
services.users: Researchers, municipalities, industry, and the public

relationships.service: Access forest management standards and guidelines

services.main_service_name: Respond to wildfires
services.name: Help with wildfire response
services.desc: Pathways for individuals and organizations to help during active wildfire response, including contract services and volunteer opportunities.

services.main_service_name: Respond to wildfires
services.name: Apply to become a wildland firefighter
services.desc: Recruitment for Alberta Wildfire's seasonal and permanent wildland firefighting positions.
services.users: Job seekers interested in wildland firefighting

services.main_service_name: Register for and manage your health care insurance
services.name: Register for the Alberta Health Care Insurance Plan
services.desc: New Alberta residents, newborns, and returning Canadians can register for AHCIP to receive publicly funded health care coverage. Registration provides a personal health number and Alberta health care card.
services.users: New Alberta residents, parents of newborns, returning Canadians

services.main_service_name: Register for and manage your health care insurance
services.name: Update your health care insurance information or replace your card
services.desc: Albertans can update their address, name, or other personal information on their AHCIP registration, and request replacement Alberta health care cards when lost or damaged.

services.main_service_name: Register for and manage your health care insurance
services.name: Check your health coverage status or claim history
services.desc: Albertans and health care providers can verify coverage status, check claim history, and confirm eligibility for insured services through online portals and phone inquiries.
services.users: Insured Albertans, health care providers, billing staff

services.main_service_name: Access supplementary health benefits and drug coverage
services.name: Apply for coverage under a government drug benefit program
services.desc: Albertans who qualify can apply for drug coverage through programs such as Non-Group Coverage, Coverage for Seniors, Alberta Child Health Benefit, or Alberta Adult Health Benefit. These programs cover prescription drugs listed on the Alberta Drug Benefit List.
services.users: Seniors, low-income adults, families with children, individuals without employer drug plans

services.main_service_name: Access supplementary health benefits and drug coverage
services.name: Look up whether a drug is covered on the Alberta Drug Benefit List
services.desc: The Alberta Drug Benefit List is a searchable directory of prescription drugs and their coverage status under government drug benefit programs. Pharmacists, physicians, and patients use it to determine coverage eligibility.
services.users: Pharmacists, physicians, patients enrolled in drug programs

services.main_service_name: Access supplementary health benefits and drug coverage
services.name: Apply for aids to daily living benefits
services.desc: The Alberta Aids to Daily Living program helps Albertans with a chronic disability or illness by providing funding for basic medical equipment and supplies such as wheelchairs, respiratory equipment, prosthetics, and ostomy supplies.
services.users: Albertans with chronic disabilities, home care clients, health providers

services.main_service_name: Find primary care and get health advice
services.name: Find a family doctor or primary care provider
services.desc: Albertans looking for a family doctor can search for physicians accepting new patients and learn about primary care options in their community, including Primary Care Networks and walk-in clinics.
services.users: Albertans without a family doctor, new residents

services.main_service_name: Find primary care and get health advice
services.name: Get advice on a health concern
services.desc: Health Link 811 is a 24/7 telephone service staffed by registered nurses providing health advice, symptom assessment, and direction to appropriate health services. Available to all Albertans.

services.main_service_name: Find primary care and get health advice
services.name: Access your personal health records online
services.desc: MyHealth Records allows Albertans to view their immunization history, lab results, medication dispensing history, and hospital visit records through a secure online portal.

services.main_service_name: Get immunizations and prevent communicable diseases
services.name: Get routine or travel immunizations
services.desc: Alberta provides publicly funded routine immunizations for children and adults through public health offices, pharmacies, and physician offices. Travel immunizations are also available. The immunization schedule covers diseases from infancy through adulthood.

services.main_service_name: Get immunizations and prevent communicable diseases
services.name: Report or learn about a notifiable disease
services.desc: Health care providers are required to report notifiable communicable diseases to the Chief Medical Officer of Health. The ministry publishes surveillance reports and provides guidance on disease prevention and outbreak management.
services.users: Health care providers, public, outbreak response teams

services.main_service_name: Get immunizations and prevent communicable diseases
services.name: Access health promotion and disease prevention resources
services.desc: The ministry provides evidence-based resources on healthy living, chronic disease prevention, smoking cessation, cannabis health effects, and other public health topics to help Albertans make informed health choices.
services.users: All Albertans, health educators, community organizations

services.main_service_name: Register or verify a regulated health professional
services.name: Register as a regulated health professional in Alberta
services.desc: Health professionals regulated under the Health Professions Act must register with their respective regulatory college. The ministry oversees the regulatory framework governing over 30 regulated health professions and their colleges.
services.users: Physicians, nurses, pharmacists, dentists, and other regulated health professionals

services.main_service_name: Register or verify a regulated health professional
services.name: Verify a health professional's registration status
services.desc: Albertans can check whether a health professional is registered and in good standing with their regulatory college. Each college maintains a public register of members.

services.main_service_name: Register or verify a regulated health professional
services.name: File a complaint about a regulated health professional
services.desc: Albertans can file complaints about the conduct, competence, or capacity of a regulated health professional with their respective regulatory college. The college investigates and may take disciplinary action.
services.users: Patients, family members, other health providers

services.main_service_name: Find or get licensed as a continuing care facility
services.name: Find a continuing care facility or home care provider
services.desc: Albertans and families can search for licensed continuing care facilities including nursing homes, designated supportive living, and home care providers across the province. Results include facility details, accommodation types, and contact information.

services.main_service_name: Find or get licensed as a continuing care facility
services.name: Apply for a continuing care facility licence or comply with standards
services.desc: Operators of nursing homes, designated supportive living, and other continuing care facilities must be licensed and meet provincial accommodation and care standards. The ministry conducts inspections and enforces compliance.

services.main_service_name: Access health data, research, and wait time information
services.name: Check surgical and diagnostic wait times
services.desc: Albertans can view estimated wait times for surgeries and diagnostic procedures by procedure type and location. Helps patients and physicians plan care and manage expectations.
services.users: Patients waiting for procedures, referring physicians

services.main_service_name: Access health data, research, and wait time information
services.name: Access health research data and apply for research funding
services.desc: The ministry supports health research through data access, funding programs, and partnerships. Researchers can access de-identified health data and apply for provincial health research grants.
services.users: Health researchers, academic institutions, health system planners

services.main_service_name: Access health data, research, and wait time information
services.name: Contact the Health Advocate for help with a concern
services.desc: The Office of the Alberta Health Advocates provides independent assistance to Albertans who have concerns about health services, continuing care, or mental health and addiction services. Advocates help navigate the system and resolve complaints.
services.users: Patients, family members, caregivers with health system concerns

services.main_service_name: Access emergency and hospital care information
services.name: Find a hospital or emergency department
services.desc: Albertans can locate hospitals, urgent care centres, and emergency departments across the province, including facility details, services offered, and contact information.
services.users: All Albertans needing emergency or hospital care

services.main_service_name: Access emergency and hospital care information
services.name: Check surgical and diagnostic wait times
services.desc: Albertans can view estimated wait times for surgeries and diagnostic procedures by procedure type and location. Helps patients and physicians plan care and manage expectations.
services.users: Patients waiting for procedures, referring physicians

services.main_service_name: Access cancer care and organ donation services
services.name: Learn about cancer care programs and find treatment centres
services.desc: Alberta provides comprehensive cancer care through regional cancer centres and community oncology clinics. Albertans can find treatment locations, learn about screening programs, and access information on cancer prevention, diagnosis, and treatment.

services.main_service_name: Access cancer care and organ donation services
services.name: Register as an organ and tissue donor
services.desc: Albertans can register their decision to be an organ and tissue donor. The provincial organ and tissue donation program coordinates donation and transplantation services across Alberta's hospitals.

services.main_service_name: Access health data, research, and system performance information
services.name: Access health research data and apply for research funding
services.desc: The ministry supports health research through data access, funding programs, and partnerships. Researchers can access de-identified health data and apply for provincial health research grants.
services.users: Health researchers, academic institutions, health system planners

services.main_service_name: Access health data, research, and system performance information
services.name: Report a patient safety concern at a health facility
services.desc: Albertans can report patient safety concerns, infection control issues, and quality of care problems at hospitals and health facilities. Reports support continuous quality improvement and regulatory oversight.
services.users: Patients, family members, health care workers

services.main_service_name: Get help from the Health Advocate with a health system concern
services.name: Contact the Health Advocate for help with a concern
services.desc: The Office of the Alberta Health Advocates provides independent assistance to Albertans who have concerns about health services, hospital care, or mental health and addiction services. Advocates help navigate the system and resolve complaints.
services.users: Patients, family members, caregivers with health system concerns

services.main_service_name: Apply for provincial nomination as a skilled worker
services.name: Apply through the Alberta Opportunity Stream
services.desc: Workers currently employed in Alberta in an eligible occupation can apply for a provincial nomination for permanent residence through the Alberta Opportunity Stream. Applicants must meet criteria for work experience, language proficiency, and education.
services.users: Temporary foreign workers and residents working in Alberta

services.main_service_name: Apply for provincial nomination as a skilled worker
services.name: Apply through the Alberta Express Entry Stream
services.desc: Candidates in the federal Express Entry pool with strong ties to Alberta or skills in priority sectors (health care, technology, policing) can receive a provincial notification of interest and apply for an Alberta nomination, boosting their federal ranking.
services.users: Federal Express Entry candidates with Alberta connections or priority skills

services.main_service_name: Apply for provincial nomination as a skilled worker
services.name: Apply through the Rural Renewal Stream or Tourism and Hospitality Stream
services.desc: Workers in rural Alberta communities or in tourism and hospitality roles can apply for nomination through dedicated streams designed to address labour needs in smaller communities and the service sector.
services.users: Workers in rural Alberta or tourism/hospitality sector

services.main_service_name: Apply for provincial nomination as an entrepreneur
services.name: Apply to start or buy a business in Alberta as an entrepreneur
services.desc: International entrepreneurs can apply for provincial nomination through the Rural Entrepreneur Stream, Graduate Entrepreneur Stream, Farm Stream, or Foreign Graduate Entrepreneur Stream by proposing a viable business plan and investing in Alberta's economy.
services.users: International entrepreneurs and investors seeking permanent residence

services.main_service_name: Apply for provincial nomination as an entrepreneur
services.name: Check your immigration application status
services.desc: Applicants to any Alberta Advantage Immigration Program stream can check the status of their application through the online portal. Processing times and application requirements vary by stream.

services.main_service_name: Get help settling in Alberta as a newcomer
services.name: Find settlement services and newcomer supports in your community
services.desc: Newcomers can find funded settlement organizations in their community that provide orientation, language assessment, employment assistance, and community connections to help them establish their new life in Alberta.
services.users: New immigrants, refugees, temporary residents transitioning to permanent residence

services.main_service_name: Get help settling in Alberta as a newcomer
services.name: Access language training and assessment
services.desc: Newcomers can access English language training, language assessment services, and bridging programs funded through provincial settlement programs. Language proficiency is key to employment and community integration.

services.main_service_name: Get help settling in Alberta as a newcomer
services.name: Get information on living and working in Alberta before you arrive
services.desc: Prospective and incoming immigrants can access guides, videos, and resources about life in Alberta including housing, health care, education, transportation, and workplace culture through the Opportunity Alberta hub.
services.users: Prospective immigrants, approved applicants preparing to move

services.main_service_name: Get your foreign credentials recognized in Alberta
services.name: Get your international qualifications assessed
services.desc: The International Qualifications Assessment Service (IQAS) assesses foreign educational credentials against Alberta standards. An IQAS certificate is often required for immigration applications, professional licensing, and employment.
services.users: Internationally educated professionals, immigration applicants, employers

services.main_service_name: Get your foreign credentials recognized in Alberta
services.name: Navigate the licensing process for your regulated profession
services.desc: Internationally trained professionals in over 80 regulated occupations can access guidance on the licensing process, connect with their Professional Regulatory Organization, and access bridging programs and financial supports to help them complete requirements.
services.users: Internationally trained professionals in regulated occupations

services.main_service_name: Get your foreign credentials recognized in Alberta
services.name: Find employment mentorship and career supports for newcomers
services.desc: Newcomers can access career mentorship programs, job search workshops, and employment bridging supports that help them understand the Alberta labour market, build professional networks, and find work matching their skills and experience.

services.main_service_name: Access multiculturalism and community inclusion supports
services.name: Apply for a multiculturalism or community inclusion grant
services.desc: Cultural and community organizations can apply for grants to support events, programs, and initiatives that promote cross-cultural understanding, celebrate diversity, and build inclusive communities across Alberta.

services.main_service_name: Access multiculturalism and community inclusion supports
services.name: Nominate an immigrant for an Alberta recognition award
services.desc: Albertans can nominate outstanding immigrants who have made significant contributions to their communities for recognition through the Alberta Immigrant Impact Awards, celebrating the achievements and contributions of newcomers.
services.users: Community members, organizations, employers wishing to recognize immigrant contributions

services.main_service_name: Report discrimination or learn about your human rights
services.name: File a human rights complaint about discrimination
services.desc: Albertans who have experienced discrimination based on race, colour, ancestry, place of origin, religious beliefs, gender, disability, or other protected grounds can file a complaint with the Alberta Human Rights Commission for investigation and resolution.
services.users: Individuals who have experienced discrimination in employment, services, or housing

services.main_service_name: Report discrimination or learn about your human rights
services.name: Learn about human rights protections and anti-racism resources
services.desc: Albertans can access educational resources on human rights protections, anti-racism initiatives, and cultural diversity. Resources include guides on protected grounds, employer obligations, and how to promote inclusive workplaces and communities.
services.users: All Albertans, employers, educators, community leaders

services.main_service_name: Track a school or health facility capital project
services.name: Check the status of a new school construction or modernization project
services.desc: School boards, parents, and communities can track the status of approved school capital projects including new construction, modernizations, and replacements. Project information includes location, scope, timeline, and current construction phase.
services.users: Parents, school boards, community members in growing areas

services.main_service_name: Track a school or health facility capital project
services.name: Check the status of a health facility construction or expansion project
services.desc: Communities can track the status of health facility capital projects including new hospitals, expansions, and major renovations. Project details include scope, timeline, construction phase, and expected completion.
services.users: Community members, health administrators, municipal councils

services.main_service_name: Bid on or participate in a public infrastructure project
services.name: Find and bid on government infrastructure tenders
services.desc: Construction firms, architects, engineers, and other contractors can find open tenders for government infrastructure projects including schools, health facilities, government buildings, and maintenance contracts. Bidding follows the Public Works Act procurement framework.
services.users: Construction companies, engineering firms, architects, subcontractors

services.main_service_name: Bid on or participate in a public infrastructure project
services.name: Resolve a payment dispute on a public works contract
services.desc: Contractors and subcontractors on public works projects can use the prompt payment and adjudication framework under the Public Works Act to resolve payment disputes. The Act sets mandatory payment timelines and provides binding adjudication for disputed invoices.
services.users: General contractors, subcontractors, suppliers on government projects

services.main_service_name: Access government property and leasing services
services.name: Buy surplus government property
services.desc: When government properties (land or buildings) are no longer needed, they are made available for sale. Interested buyers can find listings of available surplus government properties across the province.
services.users: Developers, investors, municipalities, members of the public

services.main_service_name: Access government property and leasing services
services.name: Lease space to or from the Government of Alberta
services.desc: Property owners can offer space for government lease, and government agencies can request accommodation through the ministry's realty services. The ministry manages hundreds of leases across the province for government offices and service delivery locations.
services.users: Commercial property owners, landlords, government accommodation requesters

services.main_service_name: Report a facility issue or access building services
services.name: Report a maintenance or safety issue at a government building
services.desc: Tenants, staff, and members of the public can report maintenance issues, safety concerns, or facility problems at government-owned buildings. The ministry manages building systems, safety compliance, and capital maintenance across the government facility portfolio.
services.users: Government staff in provincial buildings, members of the public visiting government offices

services.main_service_name: Report a facility issue or access building services
services.name: Access technical standards and guidelines for public buildings
services.desc: Architects, engineers, and project managers working on government infrastructure projects can access the ministry's technical standards and design guidelines for public buildings, covering accessibility, energy performance, building systems, and safety requirements.
services.users: Design professionals, project managers, construction firms working on government projects

services.main_service_name: Engage with public-private partnership and alternative financing opportunities
services.name: Learn about public-private partnership opportunities for infrastructure
services.desc: Private sector firms and investors can learn about Alberta's public-private partnership (P3) model, current and upcoming P3 opportunities, and the framework for partnering with government to deliver public infrastructure such as schools, roads, and health facilities.
services.users: Construction firms, investors, financial institutions, infrastructure developers

services.main_service_name: Engage with public-private partnership and alternative financing opportunities
services.name: Review capital plan reports and infrastructure spending accountability
services.desc: Albertans can review the provincial capital plan, annual reports on infrastructure spending, project performance, and financial accountability for public infrastructure investments. Reports are published through the ministry and budget documents.

services.main_service_name: Protect heritage resources during infrastructure development
services.name: Request a heritage resource impact assessment for a project
services.desc: When an infrastructure or development project may affect designated historical resources, heritage buildings, or archaeological sites, proponents must obtain a heritage resource impact assessment. The ministry coordinates with heritage authorities to protect significant sites.
services.users: Project proponents, developers, construction firms near heritage sites

services.main_service_name: Protect heritage resources during infrastructure development
services.name: Look up designated heritage buildings and provincial historic sites
services.desc: Albertans, researchers, and developers can look up buildings and sites designated as provincial historic resources. Designated resources have legal protection and specific requirements for any proposed alterations or nearby development.
services.users: Property owners, developers, historians, researchers, tourists

services.main_service_name: Participate in consultation on a land or resource decision
services.name: Respond to a consultation notification on a proposed project or land decision
services.desc: When a land or resource decision may affect Treaty rights or traditional uses, First Nations and Métis communities receive notification and can participate in consultation. Communities can raise concerns, request information, and propose accommodation measures.
services.users: First Nations and Métis community leadership, consultation coordinators

services.main_service_name: Participate in consultation on a land or resource decision
services.name: Submit a consultation assessment request as a project proponent
services.desc: Industry proponents and government agencies submit consultation assessment requests through the ACO Digital Service when a proposed project or decision triggers the duty to consult. The ACO determines the scope and level of consultation required.
services.users: Energy, mining, forestry, and infrastructure proponents

relationships.service: Respond to a consultation notification on a proposed project or land decision

services.main_service_name: Participate in consultation on a land or resource decision
services.name: Access consultation capacity funding as an Indigenous community
services.desc: The Indigenous Consultation Capacity Program provides annual funding to First Nations and Métis communities to support their ability to participate effectively in Alberta's consultation process, including hiring staff, reviewing technical documents, and engaging in field studies.
services.users: First Nations and Métis communities participating in consultation

services.main_service_name: Access funding for Indigenous economic and community development
services.name: Apply for a First Nations community development grant
services.desc: First Nations can apply for grants through the First Nations Development Fund for economic, social, and community development projects. The fund, sourced from First Nations gaming revenues, supports infrastructure, education, health, cultural preservation, and economic diversification.
services.users: First Nations governments and community organizations

services.main_service_name: Access funding for Indigenous economic and community development
services.name: Apply for Indigenous business investment funding
services.desc: Indigenous community-owned businesses can apply for investment funding through the Aboriginal Business Investment Fund, which provides up to $10 million per year for business ventures that create employment and economic opportunities in Indigenous communities.
services.users: Indigenous community-owned businesses and economic development corporations

services.main_service_name: Access funding for Indigenous economic and community development
services.name: Access employment and training supports for Indigenous workers
services.desc: The Employment Partnership Program supports Indigenous people in accessing training, skills development, and employment opportunities. Funding supports community-based employment projects, skills training, and partnerships between Indigenous organizations and employers.
services.users: Indigenous job seekers, training providers, employers, community organizations

services.main_service_name: Support Indigenous community safety and reconciliation
services.name: Apply for community safety funding addressing violence against Indigenous women and girls
services.desc: Indigenous-led organizations can apply for funding through the Community Support Fund for projects that address violence and promote the safety and economic security of Indigenous women, girls, and two-spirit people, responding to the Calls for Justice from the National Inquiry into MMIWG.

services.main_service_name: Support Indigenous community safety and reconciliation
services.name: Access reconciliation and cultural preservation project funding
services.desc: The Indigenous Reconciliation Initiative supports Indigenous-led cultural revitalization, language preservation, and reconciliation projects. Funding helps communities maintain and strengthen cultural practices, oral histories, and intergenerational knowledge transfer.
services.users: Indigenous cultural organizations, community groups, elders' councils

services.main_service_name: Access Métis Settlement membership and land services
