services.desc: People at risk of domestic violence can request information about a partner's violent past. Two paths: 'Right to Ask' (citizen initiates) and 'Right to Know' (police initiate). Disclosure takes approximately 4 weeks.
services.users: People in intimate partner relationships who feel at risk

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get protection from violence
services.name: Call the Family Violence Info Line
services.desc: 24/7 support line for anyone experiencing or concerned about family violence. Available in 170 languages.
services.users: Anyone experiencing or concerned about family violence

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get protection from violence
services.name: Apply for the Escaping Abuse Benefit
services.desc: Get emergency financial help to establish a household after leaving an abusive situation.

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get protection from violence
services.name: Get a Safer Spaces Certificate to break a lease
services.desc: Break a residential lease without penalty when fleeing domestic violence.

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get protection from violence
services.name: Apply for a human trafficking protection order
services.desc: Get a 3-year court order to protect trafficking survivors from their traffickers.

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get help as a victim of crime
services.name: Apply for financial assistance as a crime victim
services.desc: Apply for emergency assistance, residential security, counselling, medical treatment, and other supports. Crime must be reported to police. Apply within 2 years.
services.users: Victims of serious Criminal Code offences

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get help as a victim of crime
services.name: Submit a victim impact statement
services.desc: Prepare and submit a statement describing how a crime affected you, for the judge to consider before sentencing.
services.users: Victims of crime before offender sentencing

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get help as a victim of crime
services.name: Submit a community impact statement
services.desc: Describe the impact of a crime on your community for the judge to consider before sentencing.

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get help as a victim of crime
services.name: Request victim restitution from an offender
services.desc: Request court-ordered compensation from an offender for crime-related expenses. Must submit before sentencing.
services.users: Victims of crime where the offender has been convicted

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get help as a victim of crime
services.name: Find a victim services organization near you
services.desc: Locate a victim serving organization in your area. Four regional societies align with RCMP districts.

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get help as a victim of crime
services.name: Get reimbursed for attending court as a victim
services.desc: Get reimbursed for travel expenses to attend court proceedings as a crime victim or family of a homicide victim.
services.users: Crime victims, families of homicide victims

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get help as a victim of crime
services.name: File a complaint about victim services
services.desc: File complaints about victim serving organizations or the Victims of Crime Assistance Program.

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get help as a victim of crime
services.name: Get support as a family of a missing or murdered Indigenous person
services.desc: Access dedicated support and information through the Family Information Liaison Unit.
services.users: Families of missing or murdered Indigenous people

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Protect your rights and plan for the future
services.name: Apply for guardianship or trusteeship of an adult
services.desc: When an adult cannot make personal or financial decisions, family can apply to court for guardianship (personal decisions) or trusteeship (financial decisions). The Office of the Public Guardian and Trustee assists and acts as guardian/trustee of last resort.
services.users: Family members of adults who lack capacity

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Protect your rights and plan for the future
services.name: Get a capacity assessment done
services.desc: Have a qualified professional assess whether an adult can make decisions independently.
services.users: Families or legal representatives of adults with suspected decision-making challenges

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Protect your rights and plan for the future
services.name: Explore decision-making alternatives to guardianship
services.desc: Consider co-decision-making, supported decision-making, and specific decision-making as less restrictive alternatives to full guardianship or trusteeship.
services.users: Families of adults who need decision-making support

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Protect your rights and plan for the future
services.name: Make a personal directive or enduring power of attorney
services.desc: Create a personal directive (names an agent for personal decisions if you become incapacitated) and/or enduring power of attorney (gives authority over financial decisions).

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Protect your rights and plan for the future
services.name: File a complaint about a guardian, trustee, or agent
services.desc: Report concerns about someone acting as guardian, trustee, or agent for a vulnerable adult.
services.users: Concerned family members, community members, professionals

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Resolve a dispute outside of court
services.name: Resolve a residential tenancy dispute
services.desc: Use the Residential Tenancy Dispute Resolution Service as a faster alternative to court for landlord-tenant disputes. Claims under $100,000 within 2 years.

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Resolve a dispute outside of court
services.name: Resolve a condominium dispute
services.desc: Use the Condominium Dispute Resolution Tribunal for guided negotiation, mediation, or adjudication of condominium-related disputes.
services.users: Condo owners, condo boards, property managers

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Resolve a dispute outside of court
services.name: Participate in a restorative justice program
services.desc: Voluntary, confidential process where victims, offenders, family members, and community meet to address harm and promote meaningful resolution.

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get death investigation information
services.name: Report an unexpected or unexplained death
services.desc: Notify the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner of a sudden, unexpected, or unexplained death. Medical Investigator Unit available 24/7.
services.users: Public, physicians, healthcare professionals, law enforcement

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get death investigation information
services.name: Request death investigation documents
services.desc: Obtain documents related to a completed death investigation by the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner.
services.users: Families of deceased, lawyers, insurance companies

services.goal_name: Albertans are supported in their interactions with the justice system
services.main_service_name: Get death investigation information
services.name: Request a public fatality inquiry
services.desc: Request through the Fatality Review Board that a public inquiry be held into a death investigated by the Chief Medical Examiner.
services.users: Family members of deceased, interested parties

services.main_service_name: Participate in or understand municipal governance
services.name: Find information about your municipality's finances and services
services.desc: Albertans can access information about their municipality's financial reports, budgets, service offerings, and governance structure. Municipal financial data and service information is available through municipal websites and provincial reporting platforms.
services.users: Property owners, residents, municipal electors, taxpayers

services.main_service_name: Participate in or understand municipal governance
services.name: Understand or participate in a municipal election
services.desc: Albertans can find information about municipal elections, voting procedures, candidate information, and electoral timelines. The ministry supports municipal election administration and provides guidance on voting eligibility and participation.
services.users: Voters, candidates, municipal election officials, electors

services.main_service_name: Participate in or understand municipal governance
services.name: Appeal a municipal planning or development decision
services.desc: Property owners and affected residents can appeal municipal planning decisions, development approvals, or land use designations. The appeal process provides an independent review of municipal decisions affecting land development and use.

services.main_service_name: Understand or challenge your property assessment
services.name: Look up your property assessment
services.desc: Property owners can look up their assessed property value, assessment classification, and assessment details through online portals or by contacting their municipal assessment authority. Assessment information is used for property tax calculations and planning purposes.

services.main_service_name: Understand or challenge your property assessment
services.name: File a complaint about your property assessment
services.desc: Property owners who disagree with their assessment can file a complaint or appeal through their municipal assessment review board or through provincial appeals processes. The complaint process allows property owners to challenge assessments they believe are inaccurate.
services.users: Property owners, property managers, real estate professionals

services.main_service_name: Get a building, electrical, gas, plumbing, or fire permit
services.name: Apply for a construction or renovation permit
services.desc: Property owners and contractors can apply for permits required for new construction, additions, renovations, or alterations to buildings. Permits ensure projects comply with the Alberta Safety Codes and building standards. Applications are submitted to the local municipal authority or accredited agency.

relationships.service: Develop and administer safety codes and building standards

services.main_service_name: Get a building, electrical, gas, plumbing, or fire permit
services.name: Find your local safety codes authority or accredited agency
services.desc: Property owners and contractors can find their local safety codes authority or accredited agency responsible for issuing permits and conducting inspections in their area. The ministry maintains a registry of all authorized authorities and agencies.

relationships.service: Develop and administer safety codes and building standards

services.main_service_name: Resolve a safety codes issue or get a new home warranty
services.name: Appeal a safety codes decision or order
services.desc: Builders, property owners, and contractors can appeal safety codes decisions, inspection orders, or compliance requirements. The appeal process provides independent review of safety codes authority determinations.

services.main_service_name: Resolve a safety codes issue or get a new home warranty
services.name: File a claim under the new home warranty program
services.desc: Home buyers and owners can file claims for warranty coverage under the new home buyer protection program. Coverage includes structural defects, electrical and plumbing defects, and other major systems. Claims can be filed during the applicable warranty period.
services.users: New home buyers, homeowners, home builders

services.main_service_name: Access library services
services.name: Find a public library near you
services.desc: Albertans can find public library locations, hours, services, and programs in their community. Libraries provide free access to books, digital resources, computers, programming, and community space. The ministry supports library funding and coordination across the province.
services.users: All Albertans, students, families, seniors, community members

services.main_service_name: Get disaster recovery assistance
services.name: Apply for disaster recovery assistance after a declared disaster
services.desc: Residents and businesses affected by declared disasters can apply for financial assistance to recover from damages. Assistance programs provide funding for emergency repairs, temporary accommodation, and recovery expenses when disasters are officially declared.

services.main_service_name: Get disaster recovery assistance
services.name: Access information on emergency preparedness for your community
services.desc: Albertans can access emergency preparedness information, planning guides, and resources to prepare their families and communities for potential disasters. Information includes emergency kits, evacuation procedures, and community preparedness planning.
services.users: All Albertans, families, community leaders, businesses

services.main_service_name: Get mental health crisis support or find counselling
services.name: Get crisis support for a mental health or addiction emergency
services.desc: Albertans in mental health or addiction crisis can access immediate help through 211 Alberta (24/7, over 250 languages), Kids Help Phone (ages 11–25), and emergency services. Crisis support includes phone, text, and online chat.
services.users: Albertans in crisis, family members, bystanders

services.main_service_name: Get mental health crisis support or find counselling
services.name: Find affordable counselling for mental health or substance use
services.desc: Counselling Alberta provides affordable, accessible in-person and online counselling services with no waitlist. Sliding-scale fees ensure services are available regardless of income. Recovery Alberta also provides community-based counselling.
services.users: Adults, youth, and families seeking mental health or substance use counselling

services.main_service_name: Get mental health crisis support or find counselling
services.name: Find community mental health services near you
services.desc: Recovery Alberta and community partners deliver outpatient mental health services across the province including assessment, therapy, day programs, and psychiatric consultation. Services are available in person and virtually.
services.users: Albertans with mental health concerns, family members

services.main_service_name: Access mental health support for children and youth
services.name: Get mental health support for a child or youth
services.desc: Kickstand provides free, confidential mental health and addiction services for ages 11–25, including peer support, Indigenous wellness, and social services. CASA Mental Health offers school-based therapy and residential programs for children and youth.

services.main_service_name: Access mental health support for children and youth
services.name: Find resources for supporting a young person with mental health challenges
services.desc: Parents, caregivers, and educators can access resources, information, and support for helping children and youth manage mental health and substance use challenges, including bullying support and crisis safety planning.

services.main_service_name: Get treatment for addiction or substance use
services.name: Access addiction treatment or detoxification services
services.desc: Recovery Alberta provides free detoxification and addiction treatment through community-based outpatient programs, day programs, and residential facilities. Treatment options include medically supported withdrawal management, counselling, and opioid agonist treatment.
services.users: Albertans with substance use disorders, family members

services.main_service_name: Get treatment for addiction or substance use
services.name: Access same-day virtual addiction medicine through the opioid dependency program
services.desc: The Virtual Opioid Dependency Program (VODP) provides same-day access to addiction medicine specialists by phone for Albertans with opioid use disorder. Available 8am–8pm daily, no referral required.

services.main_service_name: Get treatment for addiction or substance use
services.name: Apply to a recovery community for long-term residential treatment
services.desc: Alberta's recovery communities provide up to one year of free, live-in treatment including opioid agonist medication, counselling, employment training, financial and housing support, and peer mentorship. Currently four communities are operational, with more planned including five in partnership with Indigenous nations.

services.main_service_name: Access harm reduction and overdose prevention services
services.name: Get a free naloxone kit
services.desc: Free naloxone kits are available at over 2,000 locations across Alberta including pharmacies, health centres, and community organizations. Naloxone reverses the effects of an opioid overdose and can save lives.
services.users: People who use opioids, friends, family members, community members

services.main_service_name: Access harm reduction and overdose prevention services
services.name: Access supervised drug consumption or overdose prevention services
services.desc: Supervised drug consumption services operate in Calgary, Edmonton, Grande Prairie, and Lethbridge, providing a safe space to use substances under medical supervision. The Digital Overdose Response System (DORS) app is also available for solo users.
services.users: People who use drugs, community members

services.main_service_name: Access harm reduction and overdose prevention services
services.name: Learn about substance risks and drug safety information
services.desc: The ministry provides evidence-based information about the risks of alcohol, cannabis, opioids, methamphetamine, and other substances through the DrugSafe program and online resources, supporting informed decisions about substance use.
services.users: All Albertans, parents, educators, health providers

services.main_service_name: Find a licensed treatment provider or report a concern
services.name: Find a licensed addiction or mental health treatment provider
services.desc: Albertans can search for licensed addiction and mental health service providers across the province, including residential treatment facilities, outpatient programs, and recovery services.
services.users: Albertans seeking treatment, family members, referral agencies

relationships.service: License and regulate addiction and mental health service providers

services.main_service_name: Find a licensed treatment provider or report a concern
services.name: Report a concern about a mental health or addiction service provider
services.desc: Albertans can report safety, quality, or conduct concerns about licensed addiction and mental health service providers to the ministry for investigation.

relationships.service: License and regulate addiction and mental health service providers

services.main_service_name: Register for and manage your health care insurance
services.name: Register for the Alberta Health Care Insurance Plan
services.desc: New Alberta residents, newborns, and returning Canadians can register for AHCIP to receive publicly funded health care coverage. Registration provides a personal health number and Alberta health care card.
services.users: New Alberta residents, parents of newborns, returning Canadians

relationships.service: Register as a regulated health professional in Alberta

services.main_service_name: Register for and manage your health care insurance
services.name: Update your health care insurance information or replace your card
services.desc: Albertans can update their address, name, or other personal information on their AHCIP registration, and request replacement Alberta health care cards when lost or damaged.

relationships.service: Register as a regulated health professional in Alberta

services.main_service_name: Register for and manage your health care insurance
services.name: Check your health coverage status or claim history
services.desc: Albertans and health care providers can verify coverage status, check claim history, and confirm eligibility for insured services through online portals and phone inquiries.
services.users: Insured Albertans, health care providers, billing staff

services.main_service_name: Access supplementary health benefits and drug coverage
services.name: Apply for coverage under a government drug benefit program
services.desc: Albertans who qualify can apply for drug coverage through programs such as Non-Group Coverage, Coverage for Seniors, Alberta Child Health Benefit, or Alberta Adult Health Benefit. These programs cover prescription drugs listed on the Alberta Drug Benefit List.
services.users: Seniors, low-income adults, families with children, individuals without employer drug plans

services.main_service_name: Access supplementary health benefits and drug coverage
services.name: Look up whether a drug is covered on the Alberta Drug Benefit List
services.desc: The Alberta Drug Benefit List is a searchable directory of prescription drugs and their coverage status under government drug benefit programs. Pharmacists, physicians, and patients use it to determine coverage eligibility.
services.users: Pharmacists, physicians, patients enrolled in drug programs

services.main_service_name: Access supplementary health benefits and drug coverage
services.name: Apply for aids to daily living benefits
services.desc: The Alberta Aids to Daily Living program helps Albertans with a chronic disability or illness by providing funding for basic medical equipment and supplies such as wheelchairs, respiratory equipment, prosthetics, and ostomy supplies.
services.users: Albertans with chronic disabilities, home care clients, health providers

services.main_service_name: Find primary care and get health advice
services.name: Find a family doctor or primary care provider
services.desc: Albertans looking for a family doctor can search for physicians accepting new patients and learn about primary care options in their community, including Primary Care Networks and walk-in clinics.
services.users: Albertans without a family doctor, new residents

services.main_service_name: Find primary care and get health advice
services.name: Get advice on a health concern
services.desc: Health Link 811 is a 24/7 telephone service staffed by registered nurses providing health advice, symptom assessment, and direction to appropriate health services. Available to all Albertans.

services.main_service_name: Find primary care and get health advice
services.name: Access your personal health records online
services.desc: MyHealth Records allows Albertans to view their immunization history, lab results, medication dispensing history, and hospital visit records through a secure online portal.

services.main_service_name: Get immunizations and prevent communicable diseases
services.name: Get routine or travel immunizations
services.desc: Alberta provides publicly funded routine immunizations for children and adults through public health offices, pharmacies, and physician offices. Travel immunizations are also available. The immunization schedule covers diseases from infancy through adulthood.

services.main_service_name: Get immunizations and prevent communicable diseases
services.name: Report or learn about a notifiable disease
services.desc: Health care providers are required to report notifiable communicable diseases to the Chief Medical Officer of Health. The ministry publishes surveillance reports and provides guidance on disease prevention and outbreak management.
services.users: Health care providers, public, outbreak response teams

services.main_service_name: Get immunizations and prevent communicable diseases
services.name: Access health promotion and disease prevention resources
services.desc: The ministry provides evidence-based resources on healthy living, chronic disease prevention, smoking cessation, cannabis health effects, and other public health topics to help Albertans make informed health choices.
services.users: All Albertans, health educators, community organizations

services.main_service_name: Register or verify a regulated health professional
services.name: Register as a regulated health professional in Alberta
services.desc: Health professionals regulated under the Health Professions Act must register with their respective regulatory college. The ministry oversees the regulatory framework governing over 30 regulated health professions and their colleges.
services.users: Physicians, nurses, pharmacists, dentists, and other regulated health professionals

relationships.service: Register for the Alberta Health Care Insurance Plan
Update your health care insurance information or replace your card

services.main_service_name: Register or verify a regulated health professional
services.name: Verify a health professional's registration status
services.desc: Albertans can check whether a health professional is registered and in good standing with their regulatory college. Each college maintains a public register of members.

services.main_service_name: Register or verify a regulated health professional
services.name: File a complaint about a regulated health professional
services.desc: Albertans can file complaints about the conduct, competence, or capacity of a regulated health professional with their respective regulatory college. The college investigates and may take disciplinary action.
services.users: Patients, family members, other health providers

services.main_service_name: Report a safety concern or get crime prevention information
services.name: Report a property or neighbourhood safety concern through SCAN
services.desc: Residents can report property crimes, neighbourhood safety concerns, or suspicious activity to police through the SCAN (Suspicious Activity) program. Reports help police identify crime patterns and deploy resources to address community concerns.

services.main_service_name: Report a safety concern or get crime prevention information
services.name: Access crime prevention resources and community safety programs
services.desc: Albertans can access information and resources about crime prevention, personal safety, home security, and community safety programs. Includes guidance on cybercrime prevention, fraud awareness, and neighbourhood safety initiatives.

services.main_service_name: Report a safety concern or get crime prevention information
services.name: Report poaching, illegal hunting, or a wildlife concern
services.desc: Albertans can report wildlife crimes including poaching, illegal hunting, or illegal trapping through the dedicated Report a Poacher line. Wildlife officers investigate reports to protect Alberta's wildlife resources and enforce hunting and trapping regulations.

services.main_service_name: Access policing oversight and complaint processes
services.name: File a complaint about police conduct
services.desc: Albertans who believe a police officer has engaged in misconduct can file a formal complaint through the independent police complaint process. Complaints are investigated and decided by an impartial body to ensure police accountability.
services.users: Members of the public with concerns about police conduct

relationships.service: Oversee policing standards and community safety programs

services.main_service_name: Access policing oversight and complaint processes
services.name: Appeal a police complaint decision
services.desc: Complainants can appeal a police complaint decision if they disagree with the outcome of their initial complaint. The appeal process provides a further level of independent review and oversight.
services.users: Individuals who have filed police complaints

relationships.service: Oversee policing standards and community safety programs

services.main_service_name: Prepare for emergencies and access alerts
services.name: Get emergency preparedness information and make a plan
services.desc: Albertans can access emergency preparedness information, including how to prepare for natural disasters, severe weather, and other emergencies. Resources include family emergency planning guides, emergency supply checklists, and guidance for vulnerable populations.
services.users: Residents, families, seniors, people with disabilities

services.main_service_name: Prepare for emergencies and access alerts
services.name: Sign up for or check Alberta Emergency Alerts
services.desc: Albertans can receive emergency alerts about severe weather, wildfires, floods, and other emergencies through Alberta Emergency Alerts. Alerts are sent to registered phones and can be checked online to stay informed of threats and emergency information.
services.users: All Albertans, residents in areas affected by emergencies

services.main_service_name: Get disaster recovery support
services.name: Apply for disaster recovery assistance
services.desc: Albertans affected by disasters (floods, wildfires, severe storms) can apply for provincial disaster recovery assistance to help with recovery costs. Applications are accepted through the disaster recovery program and assessed for eligibility and support.

services.main_service_name: Get disaster recovery support
services.name: Access wildfire or flood status information
services.desc: Albertans can check current wildfire and flood status, active incidents, evacuation orders, and impacts to communities and infrastructure. Status information is updated regularly during active incidents to keep the public informed.
services.users: Residents in or near affected areas, emergency planners, media

services.main_service_name: Access victim services and supports
services.name: Find victim services in your community
services.desc: Crime victims can access a range of support services including victim counselling, crisis support, legal information, and referrals. Services are available across the province through victim services programs and community partners.
services.users: Crime victims, family members of victims, survivors

services.main_service_name: Access victim services and supports
services.name: Access information about an offender in custody or on supervision
services.desc: Members of the public and victims can access information about offenders in provincial custody or under community supervision, including release status and conditions. Information is available to support victim safety planning and public awareness.
services.users: Crime victims, interested members of the public, community members

services.main_service_name: Access correctional and reintegration information
services.name: Visit an inmate in a provincial correctional facility
services.desc: Family members and visitors can arrange visits with inmates held in provincial correctional facilities. Visitor information includes facility locations, visiting schedules, identification requirements, and facility rules.
services.users: Family members, friends, legal representatives of inmates

services.main_service_name: Access correctional and reintegration information
services.name: Access reintegration and community supervision information
services.desc: Individuals on community supervision or release can access information about their conditions, supervision requirements, reintegration programs, and community resources. Information supports compliance and successful reintegration into the community.
services.users: Individuals on release or community supervision, support workers, community partners

services.main_service_name: Get vital records and documents
services.name: Register a birth or get a birth certificate
services.desc: Parents and guardians can register a newborn's birth or obtain a birth certificate. Birth registration is a critical identity document required for school enrolment, passport applications, and other government services.
services.users: New parents, guardians, individuals needing birth certificates

services.main_service_name: Get vital records and documents
services.name: Get a marriage certificate or register a marriage
services.desc: Couples can register a marriage and obtain marriage certificates. Marriage registration is required for legal recognition of marriage and is needed for various government, financial, and personal purposes.

services.main_service_name: Get vital records and documents
services.name: Get a death certificate
services.desc: Next of kin and legal representatives can obtain death certificates. Death certificates are required for estate administration, insurance claims, pension benefits, and legal matters following a death.

services.main_service_name: Get a driver's licence or register a vehicle
services.name: Apply for or renew your driver's licence
services.desc: Albertans can apply for a new driver's licence, renew an existing licence, or request a duplicate. Driver's licences serve as government-issued identity documents and authorization to operate vehicles on Alberta roads.
services.users: Residents, new drivers, licence renewal applicants

services.main_service_name: Get a driver's licence or register a vehicle
services.name: Register or renew your vehicle registration
services.desc: Vehicle owners can register a new vehicle, renew registration, or make changes to vehicle registration. Vehicle registration is required to legally operate a vehicle in Alberta and is needed for insurance and roadside identification.
services.users: Vehicle owners, fleet operators, new vehicle buyers

services.main_service_name: Get a driver's licence or register a vehicle
services.name: Get a learner's permit or upgrade your licence class
services.desc: New drivers can obtain a learner's permit to practice driving, and experienced drivers can upgrade to a higher licence class for commercial or specialized driving. The ministry manages Alberta's graduated licensing system.
services.users: New drivers, drivers seeking to upgrade licence class

services.main_service_name: Access registry services for businesses and personal property
services.name: Register a business name or corporation in Alberta
services.desc: Entrepreneurs can register a sole proprietorship business name, partnership, or incorporate a business in Alberta. Business registration establishes legal identity and is required to operate a business, hire employees, and access business services.

services.main_service_name: Access registry services for businesses and personal property
services.name: Search or register a personal property lien
services.desc: Creditors and lenders can register security interests in personal property, and searchers can look up registered liens. The Personal Property Registry is a searchable public database of security interests on vehicles, equipment, and other personal property.

relationships.service: Register, search, or change a personal property lien

services.main_service_name: File a consumer complaint or learn about your rights
services.name: File a complaint about a business or marketplace issue
services.desc: Consumers can file complaints about unfair business practices, fraudulent transactions, product defects, or service failures. The ministry investigates complaints, mediates disputes, and takes enforcement action against non-compliant businesses.

relationships.service: Develop consumer protection policy and enforce marketplace rules

services.main_service_name: File a consumer complaint or learn about your rights
services.name: Check for consumer alerts and enforcement actions
services.desc: Albertans can check for active consumer alerts about problematic businesses, scams, or unsafe products. The ministry publishes enforcement actions and warnings to protect consumers from known risks.

relationships.service: Develop consumer protection policy and enforce marketplace rules

services.main_service_name: Resolve a condominium dispute
services.name: File a condominium dispute for resolution
services.desc: Condominium owners and corporations can file disputes with the ministry for mediation and resolution. Common disputes include shared property maintenance, special assessments, bylaw violations, and governance issues. The ministry provides dispute resolution services.
services.users: Condominium owners, condo boards, property managers

relationships.service: Develop consumer protection policy and enforce marketplace rules

services.main_service_name: Access government information and privacy rights
services.name: Submit a freedom of information request
services.desc: Albertans can request government records and information through the Freedom of Information process. Requests provide access to government documents, meeting minutes, policy files, and other public records subject to privacy and security exceptions.

services.main_service_name: Access government information and privacy rights
services.name: File a privacy complaint
services.desc: Albertans can file complaints if they believe their personal information has been improperly collected, used, or disclosed by government or businesses. The ministry investigates privacy violations and enforces privacy protections.
services.users: Individuals whose privacy may have been violated

services.goal_name: Albertans can meet their basic needs and live with financial stability
services.main_service_name: Get help meeting basic needs
services.name: Find out what support you're eligible for
services.desc: Get help navigating available programs and determining your eligibility for income support, disability benefits, health coverage, and other assistance
services.users: Albertans unsure which programs they qualify for

services.goal_name: Albertans can meet their basic needs and live with financial stability
services.main_service_name: Get help meeting basic needs
services.name: Get income, health and other benefits for a severe disability
services.desc: Monthly income support and health benefits through AISH ($1,940/month) or the new ADAP ($1,740/month with employment income exemption). A single application determines the right program based on disability severity and work capacity.

services.users: Pension plan members, retirees, employers with pension plans

services.main_service_name: Access pension information or file a pension complaint

services.users: Restaurant and bar owners, gaming operators, cannabis retailers, non-profits running raffles

services.main_service_name: Access gaming, liquor, or cannabis licences and information
services.name: Report a concern about a licensed establishment
services.desc: Albertans can report concerns about a licensed liquor establishment, gaming operator, or cannabis retailer to AGLC for investigation. This includes concerns about overcrowding, underage sales, intoxication, or licence violations.
services.users: Members of the public, community organizations

services.main_service_name: Claim unclaimed property or access fiscal information

services.main_service_name: Access broadband and connectivity services
services.name: Check broadband availability in your area
services.desc: Albertans can check broadband availability and speed in their area through an online tool. The tool shows current high-speed internet coverage and identifies areas where expansion is planned.
services.users: Rural residents, municipalities, businesses, broadband providers

services.main_service_name: Access broadband and connectivity services
services.name: Apply for rural or Indigenous community broadband funding
services.desc: Rural municipalities, Indigenous communities, and service providers can apply for government funding to expand broadband infrastructure. The program provides grants and subsidies for network build-out in underserved areas.
services.users: Rural municipalities, Indigenous communities, broadband service providers

services.main_service_name: Use digital government services
services.name: Access your Alberta.ca account and digital wallet
services.desc: Albertans can create and manage an Alberta.ca account to access government services online, view benefits, pay bills, and manage identity information. The digital wallet enables secure presentation of government credentials.

services.main_service_name: Use digital government services
services.name: Find government services online
services.desc: Albertans can search and access online government services, applications, and information through a centralized portal. Services include licensing, permit applications, payment systems, and benefit access.

services.main_service_name: Access innovation and research funding
services.name: Apply for Alberta Innovates research or commercialization funding
services.desc: Researchers, entrepreneurs, and companies can apply for Alberta Innovates funding to support research projects, technology development, and commercialization of innovations. Funding programs include grants for basic research, applied research, and commercialization.
services.users: Researchers, research institutions, entrepreneurs, technology companies

services.main_service_name: Access innovation and research funding
services.name: Access supports for technology startups and entrepreneurs
services.desc: Technology entrepreneurs can access mentoring, incubation programs, networking events, and business support services. Programs help startups develop business models, secure funding, and scale operations.

services.main_service_name: Attract and support data centre and AI investment
services.name: Learn about Alberta's AI and data centre opportunities
services.desc: Technology companies and investors can learn about Alberta's competitive advantages for data centre and AI investment, including available infrastructure, supportive regulations, competitive electricity costs, and government incentive programs.
services.users: Technology investors, data centre operators, AI companies

services.main_service_name: Understand your privacy rights
services.name: Learn about how government collects and uses your personal information
services.desc: Albertans can access information about government privacy practices, how personal information is collected, used, and protected. Includes resources on privacy rights, data collection standards, and protection mechanisms.

services.main_service_name: Understand your privacy rights
services.name: Access cybersecurity resources and tips
services.desc: Albertans can access cybersecurity resources, safety tips, and education materials to protect themselves from online threats, identity theft, and scams. Includes information on secure passwords, phishing protection, and digital safety.

services.main_service_name: Plan travel and get help with travel problems
services.name: Access Travel Alberta's destination marketing and visitor information
services.desc: Travel Alberta markets the province as a tourism destination through digital campaigns, visitor centres, and the travelalberta.com platform. Visitors can find trip ideas, accommodations, and experiences across the province.
services.users: Domestic and international travellers, tourism operators

services.main_service_name: Plan travel and get help with travel problems
services.name: Check if a travel agent or tour operator is registered
services.desc: Alberta requires travel agents and tour operators to be registered. Consumers can verify registration status before booking travel services, providing protection against fraud and financial loss.

services.main_service_name: Plan travel and get help with travel problems
services.name: Report a travel-related complaint
services.desc: File a complaint about a registered travel agent, tour operator, or accommodation provider. The ministry investigates complaints and can take enforcement action to protect consumers.
services.users: Travellers who have experienced issues with travel services

relationships.service: Apply for a film distribution licence

services.main_service_name: Grow tourism investment and destinations
services.name: Invest in or develop a tourism product or experience
services.desc: The Tourism Investment Program provides funding to develop new and competitive tourism products, attractions, and experiences across the province, encouraging private-sector investment in tourism infrastructure.
services.users: Tourism operators, entrepreneurs, municipalities, Indigenous communities

services.main_service_name: Grow tourism investment and destinations
services.name: Propose an all-season resort development
services.desc: Under the All-Season Resorts Act, developers can propose four-season commercial tourism developments on designated Crown lands. The ministry reviews applications, conducts impact assessments, and oversees the approval process.

services.main_service_name: Grow tourism investment and destinations
services.name: Get information about all-season resort areas
services.desc: Learn about designated all-season resort areas in the Alberta Rockies, including Kananaskis, Crowsnest Pass, David Thompson corridor, and Grande Cache. Find policies, regulations, environmental requirements, and Indigenous consultation processes.
services.users: General public, municipalities, Indigenous communities, environmental groups

services.main_service_name: Collect and administer the tourism levy
services.name: Register as a tourism levy collector
services.desc: Operators of temporary accommodation (hotels, motels, B&Bs, short-term rentals) and online booking platforms must register with Alberta Tax and Revenue Administration to collect the tourism levy.
services.users: Accommodation operators, short-term rental hosts, online booking platforms

relationships.service: Check if a travel agent or tour operator is registered

services.main_service_name: Collect and administer the tourism levy
services.name: File and remit the tourism levy
services.desc: Registered operators must collect the 6% tourism levy on accommodation purchases and remit it to Alberta Tax and Revenue Administration on a monthly or quarterly basis through the online filing system.
services.users: Registered accommodation operators and online brokers

services.main_service_name: Fund sport, physical activity and recreation organizations
services.name: Apply for a sport, physical activity, or recreation operating grant
services.desc: Provincial Sport Organizations and non-profit physical activity and recreation organizations can apply for operating grants to deliver programs across Alberta. Multiple grant streams support different organizational types, from elite sport pathways to community-based active living programs.
services.users: Provincial Sport Organizations, non-profit recreation and physical activity organizations

services.main_service_name: Fund sport, physical activity and recreation organizations
services.name: Apply for a sport and recreation participation grant
services.desc: Non-profit sport and recreation organizations can apply for grants to reduce costs and increase participation, particularly among children, youth, and underrepresented groups including Indigenous youth, newcomers, persons with disabilities, and low-income families.

services.main_service_name: Fund sport, physical activity and recreation organizations
services.name: Apply for a major sport events grant
services.desc: Non-profit organizations can apply for funding to host national and international sport events in Alberta. The program supports event planning, marketing, venue preparation, and legacy initiatives.
services.users: Non-profit sport event organizers, host municipalities

services.main_service_name: Get financial help for children's sport and recreation registration
services.name: Apply for financial assistance for children's sport and recreation fees
services.desc: Alberta families can apply for up to $350 per child to help cover sport, physical activity, and recreation registration fees, reducing financial barriers to youth participation.

services.main_service_name: Fund community recreation facilities
services.name: Apply for community recreation facility capital funding
services.desc: Municipalities and non-profit organizations can apply for capital grants to build new or upgrade existing small and mid-sized recreation facilities across Alberta, including arenas, pools, gyms, and community halls.

services.main_service_name: Ensure fairness and safety in sport
services.name: Get information about the Fairness and Safety in Sport Act
services.desc: Learn about Alberta's requirements for fairness and safety in sport, including rules for sport participation categories, athlete protections, and support for mixed-sex leagues and divisions.

services.main_service_name: Ensure fairness and safety in sport
services.name: Comply with fairness and safety in sport requirements (organizations)
services.desc: Provincial Sport Organizations, post-secondary institutions, and school authorities must comply with the Fairness and Safety in Sport Act and regulation, including policies for participation categories and athlete safety.
services.users: Provincial Sport Organizations, post-secondary institutions, school authorities

services.main_service_name: Recognize contributions to sport and recreation
services.name: Nominate someone for a sport or recreation award
services.desc: Recognize outstanding contributions to sport, physical activity, and recreation in Alberta. Award programs honour athletes, coaches, officials, volunteers, and organizations.
